reviews.text: do not rent with student cribs. If i could give 0 stars i would. one room had an enormous hole in the ceiling that wasn’t fixed for months. one room had a really bad leak that they tried to convince us was just condensation because it was winter? obviously turned out to be a leak. our house was covered in black mould. worst of all, the communication was horrible and it was super rare to get any notice when work was being done, let alone the legal requirement of 24 hours. this meant that we had random men wandering round our all girl house with no warning and hugely invasive work being done, such as the kitchen counters being ripped out, the floor being ripped up, the oven and stove being out of use showers being redone etc etc, none of which we would be warned about so often we’d be left unable to cook or shower or use rooms in the house and would have no way of preparing for this. we had a pipe leak in the kitchen and rang the emergency number and literally never heard back. they never sent anyone or even followed up in any way at all. honestly could’ve ruined my uni experience, i would dissuade anyone from even considering using student cribs.
He was incredibly attentive and patient, making sure to listen to all of my concerns and ease my worries.
Student cribs provide you the most affordable accommodation with no deposit.
Very nice and friendly

Amazing agency, as a student landlords and agency's can sometimes take advantage of you but student cribs never has, any issues with housing are fixed within a day or so, particularly Geth! Geth was incredibly helpful all year, fixing everything and was very kind. Any broken furniture ect was replaced quickly. Great service!
Have lived in the same house for 3 years. Had a few issues but maintenance was great at responding to issues. Will be sad to leave this year Xx.
Cribs are really student friendly, I personally love having no deposits and their flexibility. The staff are incredibly friendly and I'm going to miss them when I move out!

I would highly recommend choosing Student Cribs. Everything has been simple from start to  finish. We have an amazing maintenance guy Mike, who always checks if there’s anything extra he can help us with and makes us feel looked after.
They are useful with repairs and everything and easy to contacts
Always responded very quickly to maintenance issues and very generous!!
Big thanks to them. They fixed the blocked drain and my bedroom door quickly and did a really good job.

Update: The morning after leaving this review i received a call from StudentCribs asking to speak about the review, considering I didn't leave a number it is a bit odd. This is also uncharacteristically quick response from student cribs as I have waited days for responses to emails, guess it's different when their public reputation is at stake.

The caller herself was kind enough but went through each point in my review and dismissed my complaints, chalking them up to system errors. I'd accept that for one instance but each of these points has happened multiple times, they have had time and warnings to fix the errors but have not.

She also assured me there would be internal review and these issues would not happen again, despite this i've received no email follow up and have actually had another man in our flat unannounced with no email, no information on the portal and none of us even asking for the repair he was here for. He had a key and let himself in despite not being a StudentCribs employee and instead being outsourced by them. This is illegal and a threat to our safety.

Review:
A terribly run company that constantly made mistakes leaving us to deal with the many consequences. Rented with them in Cardiff and I cannot recommend the experience less.

In the interest of keeping it brief here are the main issues:
1. No warning when visits to the flat will occur. I have personally woken up to three men in the flat doing work without any prior warnings. This has happened countless times. The contractors themselves are even told we will know about them. This is deceptive and wrong, we do not feel safe in the flat because anyone could walk in at anytime. This is in blatant breaking of the housing act of 1988 which states we should have 24 hours notice as well as give permission for people to enter the property.

2. Delays in getting work done. Our flat flooded and it took a long time before a maintenance worker was sent to even just look at the damage. Nothing was then done about the damage for a further month and then the minimum amount was done to fix the damage.

3. For four months in a row we were harassed for "not paying" rent. we had. we each received emails to us and then to our guarantors. The worst of it was when we would be called about the issue by a man named Raymond. from what we can tell he is the head of the finance team and he is belittling, rude and disrespectful. Frankly he should not be speaking to customers with his complete lack of understanding and respect. I have had to speak to him multiple times to sort out the rent issue despite everyone in our flat paying on time each month. This seems to have been an issue for EVERYONE IN CARDIFF who rented with student cribs. (Since the update I have been told this is because paying through the portal takes longer to process than direct debits. How this means we should get called and harassed about not paying i'm not sure)

4. communication within the company is lacking majorly. We had a flatmate swap tenancy to another person and Student Cribs completely failed to understand that. We were emailed multiple times for documentation that had been provided and asked for a document that didn't exist. This lead to the aforementioned Raymond phoning me and being again rude and demanding despite my explaining I was not the flatmate in question and couldn't help. We had emailed the documents we had to him directly before so we had all the information we had but refused to acknowledge that. He then threatened us with eviction and refused to elaborate, ending the call when I asked follow up questions.

This isn't even all that we have had to deal with in the 10 month tenancy we have had with Student Cribs. I implore everyone to look elsewhere for flats and avoid this company like the plague.

I wish I could give 0 stars but since I can't the 1 star is the for maintenance man who is always kind and professional when he shows up. His work is always done well and he has gone above and beyond for us. Shout out to him.
reviews.reply_text:
Hi Leoni,

Thank you for getting in contact. We are sorry to hear that you're experience has fallen below expectations.

We understand your Portfolio Manager has been in contact as we actively try to address all of your concerns. We appreciate your patience and understanding while this is being addressed, and we’re committed to improving your experience moving forward.

Please feel free to contact us on 020 3758 7000 if you need any further clarification. 

Kind regards,
The Student Cribs Team

reviews.text: We have absolutely loved living here. Whenever we have had any issues they would be sorted within the hour or on the same day. Mike H has been so kind to us and always reassures us if there’s anything wrong, we are so lucky to have him! Would highly recommend anyone to go with Student Cribs, it’s been a pleasure. Thanks to Mike and John:)

We’ve had a very disappointing experience with Student Cribs, mainly due to their extremely poor response and action times when it comes to maintenance and urgent issues.

The most concerning incident was being left without water for four days, during which there was little to no meaningful communication from the company. We were not given clear updates, timelines, or reassurance, which made an already stressful situation far worse. Being without water is a basic habitability issue, and the lack of urgency shown was unacceptable.

This lack of responsiveness seems to be a wider pattern. Several maintenance problems raised months ago have still not been properly addressed, despite repeated follow-ups. Issues that directly impact health and safety have been handled slowly or temporarily patched rather than properly fixed.

Overall, there appears to be a significant gap between what is promised and what is delivered in terms of maintenance and tenant support. As students, we rely on a safe and well-maintained living environment, and unfortunately, that has not been our experience.

I would strongly advise future tenants to carefully consider this before signing, particularly if prompt maintenance and clear communication are important to you.

We lived in Jesmond view, the flat is nice to be fair but beyond that there genuinely is zero positives.
Mike Cousins: Excellent maintenance man, always really polite and friendly. Really good at sorting issues quickly and efficiently - made a really big difference for our Uni house this year.

Myself and 7 fellow University of Nottingham alumni, lived in a large student dwelling in the heart of Lenton that was managed by Student Cribs.

The first day we moved in, we found carpet moths throughout the property meaning we all had to stay downstairs which caused issues. Furthermore throughout the entire time in the property we had continuous issues with our boiler requiring engineers to be regularly called out, all of whom cited that works would be required to resolve the issue. Student Cribs were not interested in fixing the issue itself which caused us to be without heating and warm water during the coldest months of the year.

Additionally, the Student Cribs team themselves were rude, they attended our property when no one was home and took photos of areas of the house that were unclean and threated us based on unevidenced violations of our tenancy agreement. Despite moving into a property that was not fit to inhabit in the first place.

Throughout the deposit return process they were condescending and rude. I am now 30, have lived in 8 landlord properties in London and if I knew then what I do now, we could have taken them to court for the entire deposit.

Avoid these guys like the plague unless you want constant headaches
They deserve any star at all.

DO NOT RENT WITH STUDENT CRIBS.
We currently live in a student house in Portsmouth and the service we have been provided with is appalling and frankly unacceptable.
The managers within the Portsmouth team are rude, dismissive and near impossible to get in contact with. During communication with them it has been alluded that we are lying about the issues and they’ve already been resolved.
We have experienced structural issues involving mould and leakages from rain and showers. Our oven was broken for 4 months. We have not had properly functioning WiFi since we moved in.
Contractors let themselves into and out of our house without notice and are often rude and dismissive of our complaints.
However, building on from a previous point the management is where the problem truly lies. Emails and phone calls are ignored and nothing is fixed. I have spoken to one property manager twice and the other I have never heard from or spoken to. We often have to speak to other offices before we can even get through to the Portsmouth team.
Living here has caused copious amounts of stress which I would not recommend to any student.

Student cribs portsmouth is poorly managed and horrible to rent with. Multiple issues have  created me and all 6 of our housemates with stress and we’re only halfway through our tenancy.

We were left without a working oven for the first 4 months of our tenancy, and reported the issue back in august. Everytime they claimed they had fixed it, the issues reappeared. The logs of maintenance were also constantly ignored and they refused to believe we logged it back in august even thought we have the receipts to prove it.

Multiple other maintenance issues were continually ignored and some which were reported back in August are still unresolved. My housemate became sick due to serious mould issues, due to the exterior wall not being up to standard, and her reports were also ignored for a while. She obviously cleaned the mould and when someone finally came and looked at it because there was no obvious mould they didn’t believe it was as serious as she had claimed. However the wall was soaking with damp.

The wifi in the house is shocking and the box was too small for 7 people. We have less than a 1GB box and although the speed check came back ok it was because only two people were home. After spending two months with barely any wifi, the people at student cribs have now told us based on reports we won’t be getting a new box.
Despite being told by Virgin we needed a new wifi box. We were told that we should not be the ones to contact them because student cribs have a business account with them, we have been told we are the ones that have to fix our wifi issues.

We pay a lot of money to have a working wifi, oven and general good housing. However we are actually paying money to be ignored and treated as if we don’t matter.

The people we speak to at student cribs constantly miss emails, are unprofessional in the sense that when we ring they don’t know anything that’s going on in the house despite emails and maintenance reports through the portal. They have no idea how to look after tenants.

We are all 3rd year uni students writing dissertations and other important exams. We don’t have working wifi and constantly under stress because we are not happy in our home thanks to student cribs.

I would not recommend renting with them. They don’t care about tenants, just the money. They say their brand is unique because it’s run for students rather than independent student housing, however Crystal Property who we rented with last year were a wonderful experience compared to the poor treatment we have received with Student Cribs.

EDIT****

Our showers are now completely out of order and we’ve been left this way for 4 days now. Contractors didn’t turn up and student cribs lied to us saying they had. We’ve had water pouring through our kitchen ceilings into electrical sockets and they didn’t care. Our roof is now also leaking as well from the rain and their advice was waiting for rain to stop. Their out of hours team are useless and so rude. They make out that you’re lying about problems even when you video call them to show them water outing through your ceilings. The ceilings are bulging with water and apparently there’s someone meant to be coming tomorrow. However based on the consistency of people coming to fix things when they say they will, our house has no hope. This is ridiculous.
Student cribs are great! Mike is excellent… always goes above and beyond with any requests and is super helpful!! Thank you!!

Do not use this housing company. I live in one of the Liverpool student houses and have requested a new front door key 4 times now within the past 2 weeks. I’m yet to have any news about getting a new one. I also had a phone call with them and they said they will log it on the system and I’ll get an email. I got neither.
Very nice house and prompt reply to any issues with the house. At York property
They are pretty good
Good and modern rooms. Quick access to public transport and grocery stores.
Would recommend, very attentive and quick to fix issues
Pretty good
Student Cribs is a nice accommodation with 24 hours excellent customer care service and prompt response to complains
Good
Apart from frequent visit and opening up one's room in one's absence with potential client, Student cribs offers good accomodation prices and convenience.

I honestly had the wrong impression about student accommodation providers before moving into Student Cribs, but my experience has been amazing. I’m really enjoying living here because the management team are very supportive and quick to respond.

Anytime there’s an issue in the house, they deal with it promptly. I once locked myself out over the weekend and they sorted it out straight away. When our freezer broke, it was replaced without any hassle.

Student Cribs respect students’ privacy, don’t cause unnecessary disturbances, and don’t add extra or unexpected charges. Overall, it’s been a stress-free and positive experience, and I would definitely recommend Student Cribs to other students.
I enjoy living the place

It’s a lovely apartment and a very comfortable place to be and live
Love it!
Good rooms and good  cleanliness  we r having availability  to near shops
Super sweet over the phone and very prompt with responses. House deposit and rent payment is so simple too!
Very helpful

Student Cribs has been a fantastic accommodation provider, offering modern, well‑maintained student housing with a genuinely supportive team behind it. The staff are friendly, quick to respond, and always willing to help, making the whole renting experience feel smooth and stress‑free. Maintenance requests are handled efficiently, communication is clear, and the properties themselves are comfortable and thoughtfully designed for student life. Overall, Student Cribs delivers a reliable, high‑quality service that makes student living feel easy and enjoyable.
Great company, easy process

A couple of maintenance issues, our bannister fell down, some of the flooring and paint job seems unfinished. But generally for a student house it does its job very well and student cribs are always relatively speedy with helping out.

I would strongly caution anyone considering renting from Student Cribs based on my experience.
I lived in one of their properties with three other young women, and throughout our tenancy we experienced repeated breaches of privacy, safety, and quiet enjoyment. Contractors and staff entered the flat without proper notice, sometimes while none of us were home. On multiple occasions, workers used our bathroom and kitchen appliances, and work was carried out while the property was left unlocked, including during a weekend when we were away.
There was constant construction noise, dust, and disruption over an extended period. Windows were incomplete for months, the living room TV was broken for over a month, and we were not informed that replacement windows would be non-opening fire windows.
We raised these concerns multiple times over the course of our tenancy, both directly and through the maintenance reporting portal. Many issues went unaddressed for months, with little to no communication or follow-up. Problems would be marked as reported but not resolved, leaving us living with broken or incomplete facilities for extended periods. This ongoing lack of response made it feel as though our concerns were consistently overlooked or deprioritised.
Hygiene and safety were also serious issues, with bathrooms left unsanitary and even blood found in shared living spaces, which was extremely distressing and made us feel unsafe in our own home.
If you value privacy, safety, timely maintenance, and clear communication, I would strongly recommend looking elsewhere.

Hi, do not use this service, been with them the last few months had multiple things broken in the flat on arrival, the kitchen door handle broke (which is also a fire door) and even though they are aware of this they haven't fixed it in weeks. Tons of little things broken in the apartment freezer kitchen cabinets etc. no windows open on the front side of the flat which leaves two people in my flat with only vents to air their rooms out. Finally they do not have any secure locations to leave mail/post, I have had  4 hello fresh boxes stolen as well as expensive prescription medication, on top of that, they refused to help me by investigating the cctv, what is the point of having cctv if when things are stolen directly in its sight it's not used. All in all for the prices they're charging they have been treating us disgracefully and I warn you not to use this company as they do not care about looking after their tenants in the slightest.
reviews.reply_text:
Hi Jake, 

Thank you for taking the time to share your feedback with us. We’re sorry to hear that your experience hasn’t met expectations and we appreciate you bringing these concerns to our attention.

While we were not previously aware of all the points you’ve raised, we take this feedback very seriously and understand how frustrating this must have been. Please be assured that we are reviewing the matters raised and are keen to understand the full context so we can address them appropriately.

Your concerns are now being looked at through our formal complaints process, and a member of our team will be in touch with you today to discuss this further. We appreciate you raising this and giving us the opportunity to resolve the matter.

Thanks, 
The Student Cribs Team

reviews.text:
My Experience in Student Cribs

My experience in student cribs was both challenging and rewarding, and it taught me important lessons about independence, responsibility, and living with others. Staying in student cribs was very different from living at home, and it pushed me out of my comfort zone in ways I did not expect.

When I first arrived, everything felt unfamiliar. I had to share space with other students who came from different backgrounds and had different habits. At the beginning, this was difficult because I was used to my own routines and personal space. Simple things like managing noise, sharing facilities, and respecting one another’s schedules required patience and understanding. However, as time went on, I learned how to adapt and communicate better with others.

Living in student cribs also taught me independence. I had to manage my time, take care of my belongings, and make decisions on my own. There was no one constantly reminding me what to do, so I learned the importance of self-discipline. Balancing schoolwork with daily responsibilities was not always easy, but it helped me grow more organized and responsible.

One of the best parts of my experience was the sense of community. Being surrounded by other students made it easier to make friends and share experiences. We supported each other academically and emotionally, especially during stressful periods like exams. These interactions helped me improve my social skills and learn the value of teamwork and cooperation.

In conclusion, my experience in student cribs was a significant part of my personal growth. Although there were challenges, the lessons I learned about independence, respect, and community made the experience worthwhile. It prepared me for future responsibilities and helped shape me into a more confident and mature individual.
My experience here is superb. I like the settings, and the neatness of the environment. And I like as the apartment is conducive for everyone.

Student cribs are terrible at responding to maintenance issues, and our landlord doesn’t answer our messages and constantly lies to us about getting things sorted.
The first issue we had was the freezer not working, the freezer itself was obviously old and needed replacing as there was leaking and was cracked up. All of our food went off and they said it would be responded to within 5 days, it took us over a month before it was finally replaced, and the only way we could get it sorted was by our housemate leaving a google review to which the landlord had told her to take the review down.
We also have been having on going issues with mould in certain bedrooms with no replies to this.
We now have a washing machine that does not work, it refuses to start and the maintenance request we put in over a week ago was replied to with urgent 5 day response. we were then given a date for maintenance to show up and nobody came, our housemate has attempted to reach out to the landlord who blatantly ignored her message. We are now left with no washing machine. Student cribs do not provide adequate maintenance service.
Excellent about all their services.I really recommend living in student cribs
Smooth, quick and friendly service. They are responsive and helpful, which made moving in really simple for me. Would definitely recommend :)
Comfortable living set up
Good service overall
The place is quiet , it has a fire alarm in case of a fire to move out.
Really enjoyed staying with student cribs, most if not all issues were resolved when brought to their attention. Would reccomned
I’ve lived in student cribs properties for 2 years and it’s been a really good experience. As a student it’s a significantly easier experience compared to living with a private landlord!
Lovely homely space! Good value for money and super quick maintenance response!
Good and fix everything asap

I had a wonderful experience with student cribs . The property was clean, well-maintained, and they made sure everything was comfortable throughout my stay. The check-in process was smooth, and all amenities were in perfect working condition.
I truly appreciated the professionalism and customer care. I would definitely recommend student cribs to anyone especially students looking for a reliable and pleasant place to stay!
Rented from SC in 2nd year of uni and the maintenance team are really quick and the house is lovely, we’ve had minimum issues
This is a nice place and am enjoying my stay
Really helpful and easy to move in when needed!
It’s a lovely and homely place

We have had ongoing issues with our Student Cribs house since July, and despite reporting everything through the portal multiple times, many problems still haven’t been resolved. This includes mould in one of the rooms and a leak in the roof, both of which are serious issues that we expected to be dealt with promptly.

Recently, someone was sent out to look at a leak coming from a toilet. While they were there, they called a member of the Student Cribs team and put them on speaker. The staff member immediately started complaining about my friend, saying things like “this girl keeps sending me emails”, even though the only reason we had emailed was because the portal requests weren’t being dealt with.

My friend (who wasn’t even in the house last week when they claimed someone came to “fix” things) was then put on the phone and spoken to very rudely. She was told to “stop emailing” because the issues were “resolved”, even though they very clearly are not. The mould is still there, and the leak has not been sorted. When my friend explained that we have been using the portal but nothing was being done, the staff member continued to blame her and even said, “If you actually communicated with your friends you would know whether the issues were still there.”

The tone and attitude were completely unprofessional, and the way my friend was spoken to was so harsh that it actually made her cry after the call. No student should be treated like that for simply trying to get essential issues in their home resolved.

Overall, we’re extremely disappointed with the lack of action, lack of communication, and frankly the lack of basic respect from certain members of staff. We just want a safe, liveable home, and after months of trying to get things fixed, we still don’t have that.
Student Cribs is actually a place to be and comfortable, they are fast in responding to issues too.
Flexible work.

I’m currently living in this accommodation and my experience so far has been excellent. The environment is clean, peaceful and well maintained, which makes it a comfortable place to stay. The management team and staff are very responsive and approachable, any issues or questions I’ve raised have been dealt with quickly and professionally. My room is spacious and tidy with all the necessary amenities to make daily living easy.The location is very convenient with easy access shops, transport links and other essential services. Overall I’m happy with my stay so far and would confidently recommend this accommodation and agency as I’ve always done to anyone looking for a comfortable and reliable place to live.

Really happy with the house we have, lots of space and it is really nice to be honest.
The only issues we did have were a burned out socket and some missing lightbulbs when we first moved in but they were sorted out extremely quickly by maintenance.
Lovely house and amazing managers and maintenance team
Great house, student cribs very attentive
Amazing , really good service
Reliable
I’ve had a great experience.
Very good service
Was really easy to find and such a lovely place with everything nearby. Will extending my stay!

Had a brilliant experience with student Cribs they helped us find a lovely house very quickly and efficiently and the lovely James pops over whenever we need him to sort out our issues big and small they also gave a lovely present to us on our birthday !!! would 100% recommend for signing a house in the future
Easy to communicate with, always helpful when having talks with the council too.
Best student housing company I’ve used in my 2 years being here
When you contact them they’re extremely good at getting back to you
Best letting agent I have used in Leeds in all 4 years i’ve been here
Very good maintenance and quick to fix any issues . Really nice interiors too- We love our student cribs house
pretty good although maintenance request waiting time could be shorter. otherwise no complaints
Really good experience. All maintenance issue are always solved quickly and good communication.

Student cribs are very ideal landlords. They answer to all of the issues we put on the portal. However sometimes their response takes longer than ideal. But overall, can’t fault my experience with them
Great letting agency for students, lot of work taken out of your hands. Friendly staff and efficient maintenance team. Reasonable rent, water and internet sorted by them. Would recommend.
Helpful staff, always quick to resolve any issues.
We have really enjoyed living in our house and have been very supported by the team all year :)
Awesome experience while living at student crib accommodation at Huddersfield, I really enjoyed my stay that I don't even want to leave, but had to because I am done with school.

Excellent customer service and very quick response to all tenants queries and concerns
The best in Stoke-on-Trent

I lived at 31 Guildford street for a year and had a great experience. The apartment was clean, maintenance was responsive, and the location was convenient. The management team was professional and helpful throughout my stay. I’d definitely recommend it to others!”
Super lovely home and a staff that are always eager to help with maintenance and ensuring the property is well kept
Great house. Good maintenance team
Very good
It's been a great year at the cribs.

Student cribs is such a terrible student company. I have moved into the property of 23 Manners Road and we have had no end issues. Since moving into the property, it was not been adequately cleaned.

The main issue is the  bathroom. There has been several blockages in the past leaving stagnant and dirty water, which has been making us ill.  The first time we discovered this it took 5 weeks for it to be fully sorted which is unacceptable.  It is profoundly disrespectful that the bathroom has been left in conditions. We have been blamed before for the blockages in the bathroom even though it has been happening since we’ve moved in.

Secondly, the kitchen and sofa has been in a terrible  condition. Half of the hobs don’t work in the kitchen and the oven and we have constantly reported these issues with barely any form of progress. There are also some kitchen cupboards that are broken and appliances that are old and dirty such as the air fryer.

The  fridge has been leaking for weeks and the dinning room chairs were broken when we moved in and still haven’t been replaced. Heavens forbid if someone falls from one of these chairs they could seriously injure themselves.

The sofa looks 100 years old, the seats are sunken it and is very dirty, it has not been replace despite being reported. Every time we report something it gets left for over a week and we have to constantly remind them.

I am throughouly frustrated with the level of disregard to our student welfare and wellbeing. It feels as if student cribs are taking advantage of students and their money.
reviews.reply_text:
Hi Angeline,
 
We are sorry to hear that your experience hasn't been as positive as it should be, and appreciate you sharing your concerns. 
 
To update you on the points raised, a new Saniflo system was installed on 24th January, as it had been blocked by items that shouldn’t be disposed of in the unit. We’ll share some guidance again to help prevent future issues, and of course, if you experience any further problems, please do let us know.
 
We’ve ordered a new oven for the property, as unfortunately after initial assessment, repairs weren’t possible.

The fridge issue reported on 25th February was attended to on Friday and resolved, which falls within our standard response time. The toilet has also been further assessed, following the concerns raised in your images, but we were unable to find any blockages. Our team has also attended to the chair repairs and we’ll be arranging for someone to assess the sofa shortly.
 
With regard to the air fryer, this isn’t something that we provide in our properties. Please let us know if this belongs to another tenant - if not, we’d be happy to arrange for its removal.
 
Your Portfolio Manager has been in touch, and we hope this has helped to address any concerns. However, if there’s anything else we can do to support you, please don’t hesitate to contact us on 0203 758 7000.
 
Thank you, 
The Student Cribs Team

reviews.text:
The accommodation is shockingly bad. We have had no end of issues since moving into the property. I am still living in the property and it is just getting worse.

Upon moving into the property the place was dirty (it obviously had not been cleaned since the previous tenants moved out) my room was especially dirty with nails on my desk and stains on the desk and walls. My blind was also broken. These are things that should be checked and replaced before new tenants move in.

Most appliances in the kitchen do not work and they will not replace them. Multiple rings on the hob have been broke since we moved it yet have not been fixed. The microwave is faulty (and very old it is falling apart).

The biggest issue I have had is the bathroom - the bath, toilet and sink are blocked, I contacted the maintenance team to repair this who came out a week later to say there were no issues. Around a week later they all blocked again and we have reported the issue over 3 weeks ago now and no one has said anything about it. It is ridiculous. The water is stagnant and dirty and could make us ill. 6 people are also having to share 1 toilet.

Avoid booking with student cribs, they only care about money and do not care about student welfare.

reviews.text:
I had a deeply disappointing experience with Student Cribs, which completely destroyed my confidence in their professionalism and ability to properly address tenant concerns. From the start, there was a major miscommunication about the tenancy length and contract terms. While they acknowledged it as their mistake, instead of offering a resolution or adjustment, they simply stuck to their new decisions without regard for my concerns. It felt as though they were dismissing my situation entirely, making it clear that I had no choice but to accept their terms or walk away.

After paying my retainer for an individual contract with a six-month lease, I received an email from Student Cribs stating that the landlord had changed their mind and wanted to switch the contract to joint and several liability with my flatmate. They requested my consent before sending the contract. I did not consent, as I wasn’t comfortable being liable for a tenant I didn’t know, and made it clear I would only agree if I wasn’t responsible for their rent or faults. Despite this, they ignored my email and sent the contract anyway, without responding. Upon reviewing the contract, I found several concerning terms, including a change to an over 7 month lease, which wasn’t what I had agreed to. I emailed them again, pointing this out and explaining that I had never consented. Student Cribs briefly acknowledged their mistake but were dismissive, refusing to address my concerns or make a resolution, insisting that I accept the new terms. Despite their clear miscommunication and false advertising, they wouldn't budge, leaving me with no choice but to request a refund due to their lack of accountability and care.

What made this worse was the poor communication throughout the process. Despite raising clear concerns, my emails were only partially addressed, forcing me to follow up repeatedly. Even after re-quoting ignored questions and explicitly asking for answers, parts of my requests were overlooked, and I continued to receive selective responses. It became exhausting and unprofessional to have important concerns ignored. Eventually, I reached a point where I had to give up, ultimately requesting a refund and cancelling due to feeling dismissed and frustrated by their lack of clear communication, accountability, and attentiveness.

Overall, this experience not only wasted my time but also left me under immense pressure, as I had less than two weeks before my move across the world, a situation already filled with stress, and then to have to find new accommodation on such short notice due to their mistakes only added even more strain. It destroyed any trust I might have had in their services. If this is how they treat tenants before move-in, this has left me concerned about how issues would be managed during the tenancy.

I strongly advise prospective tenants to carefully reconsider engaging with Student Cribs, as their disregard for tenant concerns and selective responses to queries were extremely frustrating. I also hope that the Student Cribs team can improve, as this issue was not limited to a single team member; it involved multiple individuals who were part of the email chain and contributed to my negative experience through their poor communication and lack of care.
Hattie has been exceptionally helpful indeed - helped make the whole process both seamless and smooth . All hugely appreciated.
Dirty, damp, broken furniture, unsafe, broken appliances and the students have not even moved in. The company appears to be blaming the students themselves. Laughable. Avoid
reviews.reply_text:
Dear Andy,
 
We are sorry to see your review. We have a Maintenance Operative attending the property tomorrow to treat the condensation build up and attend to the furniture & appliance jobs. 

We apologise for any confusion or stress caused in our communication with regard to the condition of the property - in the event that an issue is raised regarding the condition of one of our properties, our initial practice would be to cross reference against the inventory that was completed prior to the move in date, so that we can ascertain if there had been subsequent damages or not. In this case, there were some items that were not consistent with the condition of the property when the inventory was completed. With this, we would like to arrange for the Portfolio Manager to attend the property in the coming 48 hours to meet and review with the tenants in person. 
 
We genuinely hope that the tenants have a positive experience during their tenancy, and look forward to alleviating any concerns you or the tenants may have moving forwards. A member of the customer experience team will be giving you a call in the next 24 hours, but if you would like to discuss any further in the meantime, please give us a ring on 0203 758 7000.
 
Thank you, 
The Student Cribs Team

reviews.text:
Decent flats but communication has been poor throughout. Moved in with my boyfriend to the Leicester branch in September 2022. I'd booked key collection at the property weeks in advance. A day before I received a call telling me I need to reschedule key collection for another day. I had already arranged with my parents to take me up that day. In the end the keys were left in a very exposed area, and could've been taken at any point during those 24 hours. When we arrived at the flat, there was paint all over the floors, hinges coming off cupboards and black mould in areas of the bathroom. I asked the property manager imminently about this and she said it would be sorted out quickly: this never happened. Complaints and other maintenance issues recorded on the student portal have either taken a long time to fix or in some cases have not been fixed at all. At one point just before Christmas, someone came to the property to check the locks. She did not lock the door properly and as a result I was locked inside my flat for an hour. Luckily I am sharing with my partner and he rushed back to let me out. This meant I missed an important lecture. There have been other issues with communication in regard to laundry, bins etc. We both feel like we are paying extortionate amounts and not getting our money's worth.
Overall poor experience. Would not recommend or go back again.

Student cribs have been very helpful throughout the entirety of housing process. When I had complications with people leaving my contract and different people joining, they were clear and concise with what that process involved.

reviews.text: I found a room on spareroom, after leaving a message I received a phonecall same day to arrange a viewing. I spoke with a guy called Tom, he then sent me an email confirmation of my viewing and the address. Great. When I arrived at the property, he never showed up. I wasted time getting there after work and waiting an extra 30 minutes outside. I tried calling their office number but it was out of hours. I called back the next day and spoke to some girl that said he had canceled the viewing. Atleast he could of notified me about this and it would of been okay. Really unprofessional. The girl said she will leave a message for him to call me back, which I expected, he never did. Further more, after looking at their website, "student cribs" I found the same property as I did on spare room but it was a different address. Suspicious.
reviews.reply_text:

Thanks,
The Student Cribs Team

reviews.text:
AWFUL SCAM ARTISTS
Student cribs Manchester is a disgrace a pure scam would not recommend. We pay £130 a week for what? These con artists promote the flat 'features' on their website none of these were found in the flat staff had no idea what we were talking about. Flat was dirty on arrival my room had black mould on the ceiling which is extremely dangerous. I contacted them 3 times about this I believe it took them 3 weeks to sort it. We had to contact them many times about giving every tenant a set of keys for the flat as they weren’t provided when we moved in. The drive was covered in rubbish left from pervious tenants resulting in the council trying to fine us. The finance team are appalling they took my rent payment out TWICE resulting in me being stuck with no money for 24 hours. I could not use my card as it had been blocked, I couldn’t get to uni or buy food. As the payment was taken twice my arranged overdraft was exceeded and the bank, were threatening to charge me interest on this. This situation was highly stressful and caused distress for me and my flat mates. When I contacted the finance team, they had no remorse for what happened and refused to take the responsibility. This happened again last rent payment so clearly, they do not care about us students. All the tvs in bedrooms were broken it took them 4 months to replace my TV. The boiler has broken many times leaving us with no heating or hot water for days in winter when we contact them the staff are rude and unhelpful. They even tried to make us fix our own boiler because they didn’t want to send someone out to fix it. They also give less than 24 hours before entering the flat this has happened countless times. This is scary for us as we sometimes find people in our flat, we had no knowledge were coming round. As an all women flat we find this dangerous for obvious reasons. The windows in the flat are extremely hazardous they slam down when opened resulting in the wall falling through underneath it. This is such a death trap that one window was locked shut by maintenance as it was deemed unsafe. This means one of our fire exits is now blocked which is surely illegal?

reviews.text: Admin wise so far a joke

It was impossible to contact the local rep who was meant to be in charge locally.  She ignored emails, calls & messages, & only rang back after I contacted the London office twice.  Then she didn't send the promised email & returned to being uncontactable.

Any contact had to be through the London Office, and phone calls and emails are usually not answered or returned.

Avoid this company.  They might be fine if you have no problems but if you do they are worse than useless.

reviews.text: No urgency whatsoever in returning the deposit even after over 7 weeks.
reviews.reply_text:
Hello Harrison,

We are sorry to see your feedback and we do understand your frustration.

Student Cribs do not usually require deposits. As your property was taken over by Student Cribs after your tenancy started, we inherited your deposit from your previous landlord and there were unforeseen delays which delayed release by your Deposit Protection Scheme.

Our team have worked to resolve this, and your deposit was released on Tuesday Aug 17th, just 2 working days after the deposit was released by your DPS.

Thanks,
The Student Cribs Team

reviews.text: Terrible. I wouldn't recommend at all. Student cribs are incredibly unprofessional and caused way more stress than necessary. We were never made aware of when work was taking place, 2 weeks before our dissertation was due workers began working on our roof causing a lot of noise. People were constantly coming in and out of the house for work and inspections, which was annoying and caused a lot of anxiety in the house.  Constant issues with water and heating, as well. We were overcharged massively. When I signed my contract it was for £550, next thing I know I'm paying almost £700 a month for one room! Please, please, PLEASE, do not use student cribs. It's not worth your money whatsoever.

The new maintenance guy Shaq is really down to earth, extremely helpful, and is the best part of our experience renting 👍 Dealing with student cribs themselves is quite a long and painful process to get anything sorted in your house, unless you owe them money then they seem to be on the ball. When we moved into the property the garden was overgrown and wasn't usable even though student cribs had told us they were going to sort it, they then "Sent someone round". No one came to sort it out and we had to clear it ourselves, they then told us this was just since the last tenants which I find hard to believe. There is a video of it below. Make sure you don't use Blu tac or LED's in your room as someone I know was charged £120 per room to be repainted which is ridiculous. Our Wifi was atrocious all year. There were many times when they didn't give us the 24 hours notice before entering the property. Would not rent from them again, overpriced house and poor communication. I'm convinced the good reviews on here are because of the maintenance managers asking for them.

reviews.text: Absolutely awful. Through Covid, I wasn't even living there yet they were charging us all, the finance team were useless and didn't even help with the situation. Secondly the lady that cam rit check the housing used to scream at the top of her lungs. Thirdly, the fire alarm went off with the tiniest bit of smoke. Fourth, the house outside was so bad we got rats that they tried to charge us for. Fifth, they ignore all calls saying they'll call back and don't. Same with emails. Sixth, they just don't care about anything but money, I haven't been able to pay £750 upright and given me 3 days or they're sending debt collectors. They never sent me a warning but claimed they did. Absolutely disgusting house with mould and broken locks on the doors. DO NOT USE THIS DREADED COMPANY

Absolutely awful. The only reason I'm even giving 1 star is because I can't give 0. I'm also convinced all the good reviews are written by staff to keep their rating up. From the get go the service has been massively unprofessional. When in the contract stage, they attempted to charge us more than was advertised, and wen we called to question it, they tried to convince us that was the correct price - we had to send them screenshots from the websites to prove that they were wrong and get the rent in the contract put back to what it should have been! Since moving in things have gotten no better. For starters they very often don't give us 24hrs warning before people come into the house, leaving us, a house of 4 girls, in vulnerable positions They also definitely skimp in sorting out maintenance issues. When we first moved in we noticed mice droppings and reported it so that we could get some pest control. They assured us they had sorted the rodent problem, but a few months later our boiler exploded and leaked out a mixture of water, droppings, and dead mice!! I have also woken up to hear mice scratching up my carpet, and have seen mice running about in the living room - so they clearly did not sort out the rodent problem at all. The wifi they provide us with is also extremely temperamental and has a tendency to drop out which leads me to believe they opted for the cheapest option - not ideal in 2020/21 when uni is all online!! I truly believe this company does the absolute bare minimum in order to scrape by, and then either write their own good reviews or bribe people to leave good reviews to keep unaware students using them for houses. If I can give any advice to students looking to get a student house, it is to avoid student cribs at all costs!!!
reviews.reply_text:
Hi Megan, thank you for your review and we are sorry to see that your experience has fallen below expectations. I understand that have been in contact with your Property Manager who has resolved your feedback.

We have been  unable to identify the issue around rent pricing – from our records the advertised rent matches with the contracted amount. We can confirm that our reviews are authentic – fortunately the vast majority of our tenants are extremely satisfied with their Crib and maintenance support, however we appreciate on occasion negative experiences do happen and we work hard to resolve these.

We understand that pest control have attended multiple times shortly after you reported this - terraced houses are susceptible to pests, but maintaining the Crib in a tidy condition and attendance from pest control does mitigate this. Regarding WIFI, your Property Manager contacted the provider when she became aware of this, but they advised your Crib was already on the package with the highest available speeds.. We would also like to apologise on behalf of the contractor who carried out a recent Fire Risk Assessment and did not give appropriate notice – they are looking into why this occurred and we agree this is unacceptable. While our team consistently give at least 24 hours notice via email if they must attend the property, if this were to occur again you can deny access and alert our team to the unscheduled visit as feeling comfortable and secure in your Crib is of utmost importance to us.

If you do have further concerns or feedback please do reach out via phone: 0203 758 7000

Thanks, The Student Cribs Team

If you do have any further queries or feedback please don't hesitate to call our office: 0203 758 7000. Kind Regards, The Student Cribs Team

reviews.text: We lived in a property of Student Cribs September-March 2020 due to COVID-19. Students still had to pay rent but this company said they'd refund us our utilities as we weren't living there. However, I moved out March 15th 2020 and our contract ended July 1st 2020. Student Cribs stated they needed 3 months (till October 1st) to collect data on how much we were owed. Then a further 6 weeks to process a refund. However, they have 9 days left to refund me and the 6 weeks will be up. They have ignored my emails since AUGUST! I have had to message via instagram for any response who had said due to COVID the refund will take longer than the 6 weeks stated. I have no email confirmation of how much I'm being refunded or when... considering students still paid rent I have no idea how COVID effects a refund... absolutely appalling service. I would not advice going with this company.
reviews.reply_text: Hi Hannah, thank you for your feedback. I understand that you have spoken to a member of our accounts team to clarify the utilities refund amount, which was sent within the outlined timeline. If you have any further feedback or queries, please call our London office who will be happy to help: 0203 758 7000. Thanks, The Student Cribs Team

reviews.text: DO NOT TRUST STUDENT CRIBS to replace a tenant if the original tenant does not move in! Three paying tenants who lived at 17a Larkdate Street all got summoned to court because the 4th student who students cribs found for the apartment in the first place. The three students didn’t even know this boy but trusted Student Crib for finding him. When he didn’t pay his share for the whole year  Student Crib expected the other boys to pay. Student Crib are taking absolute no responsibilities for the 4th tenant not paying their share.  Surely the guarantor of the non paying tenant needed to cough up not the other paying students!!! Why was the guarantor not being hounded! What’s the point in having a guarantor in the first place?  Why was this boy not evicted within the year for not paying? The  three students who have never missed a payment were penalized for this boy!! In these circumstance Student Crib needed to hold their hands up for not doing a credit check on this boy and his guarantor. There needs to be some protection for the good guys paying their rent on time without fail. Student Crib you have to learn from this. You must start doing credit checks on the guarantors if you can’t do it on the student themselves!! I think deep down you know your company stuffed up on this occasion!  The stress you have put these boys and their families through because you got this boy in is just unbelievable! My advice to you is if someone drops out let it up to the students to find that extra person not you because you obviously don’t care who you put in there!!
reviews.reply_text:
Hi Nicola, We’re sorry that you have had a negative experience and thank you for the feedback.

The tenants were given the option to find a replacement tenant for the drop out, or to take a prospective tenant that was sourced via our advertising channels. We offer the latter option to help students who have signed a contract with their friends but are then left with empty rooms due to late drop outs, but in no way do we influence the decision or advocate the choice of the tenant. This decision is 100% up to the tenants. Replacement tenants and guarantors undergo the same checks as all other tenants and guarantors.

The contract was issued to all tenants and guarantors twice (once initially and then again during the replacement process). Our contract is industry standard; a sample is available for anyone to review in the FAQs section of our website. As with all contracts it is advisable to seek independent legal advice if unsure of anything.

Joint and Several contracts are standard across the industry in cases where a group of tenants sign up for a whole property. As is standard, in the unfortunate event of a tenant dropping out after the contract is signed, the contract remains in place.  As of March 2019 we have further extended our guarantor checks.

Of our +3,000 tenants each year, legal proceedings due to failure to pay are very rare and are a last resort. The tenant and guarantor who initially failed to pay were consistently contacted from the first instance of failing to pay (from 06/11/2018 to 13/5/2019), and all other tenants and guarantors were also notified and consistently contacted throughout the same period (1st communication shared with the group on 20/11/2018). Unfortunately it is the case that if tenants do not pay rent as outlined in the contract, then the outstanding amount will be pursued. In this case, an objective third party ruled that the tenants had failed to pay and were responsible for the outstanding amount.

If anything above is unclear or you have further questions, please do call our office: 0203 758 7000

Thanks, The Student Cribs Team

reviews.text: 17a larkdale street. After hearing how Student Cribs treated my god son and his friends when they paid their rent in full for the year was truly shocking. They only seemed to chase payment from the boys and the guarantors that actually paid their rent and not the tenant that dodged payment for the whole year!

If you would like to discuss this further please do contact our office and we will assist in any way we can: 0203 758 7000. Thanks, The Student Cribs Team

reviews.text: At the start I thought this was definitely the most convenient house I've had while at uni, with a cool interior, 'all bills included' and a chromecast. But by March we'd had several letters from SSE asking to pay the gas and electricity bills which was Student Crib's responsibility, we emailed and called them repeatedly just to be told 'it's sorted', when it clearly wasn't. By April we'd been visited by debt collector and sent a pay as you go card for our bills when obviously they still weren't bothering to pay the bills. When we've reported problems they've always been handled relatively quickly, so didn't understand why they were being so poor with paying the bills which is a huge pain and not what you need at university.

Finding the ideal house with a group of friends can be a tricky task when every person has their own preferences – luckily finding our Student Crib in Liverpool for second year made sure to accommodate to every student’s needs. For many, having a room on the ground floor is the least preferable of the options of all the given bedrooms in the house, however with the addition of a huge en suite, this was the deal-breaker for me, which I found very comfortable to live in.
Unlike many student houses that tend to have as many bedrooms crammed into the floor plan as possible, our Crib was a spacious one. The kitchen, living room and three bathrooms were all open enough to relax and get cosy in. With the expanse of this space, made it a great social hub for our friends to come over and chill or host before a night out.
With tech devices such as a personal televisions in each room and the soundbar attached to the TV in the living room, added extra touches of comfort and luxury for our duration of staying at the house. As well as this, it was nice to know that the furniture including beds, drawers and wardrobes were sturdy enough to hold the years supply clothes and supplies into without them collapsing on us!
Any general maintenance issues – minimal as they were – were dealt with promptly and effectively. Our maintenance manager Gary was friendly and quick to respond, as well as the helpline to the SC office. Not to mention having a tool box at the ready in the house was something we didn’t realise we would need so much until finding it at the ready for use – it is these additions that the company has already thought of to put inside in the property, which makes living in a Crib convenient as well as comfortable.

My experience with them was awful. We booked a viewing online and went there on Sunday, no one showed up. It was cold and we waited an hour, called twice and they told us twice (two different people) that they would call us back. I emphasised we are freezing and nobody called us back. We went home and called them the next day. They said they will make sure someone comes to the next viewing. We went, waited 20mins, called them and they said they called to cancel but I didn’t receive any contact. They also told us they’ve already let the house to a few people so me and my friends couldn’t get the place by the time we wanted to see it. Didn’t have a viewing, wasted time and money and energy only to find out the place was let out. We went home upset, disappointed and would never use them again. They really need to improve their service! Not professional at all, wouldn’t recommend unless you’re prepared for disappointment.
reviews.reply_text: We are so sorry that you have had a bad experience with Student Cribs, the viewing representative not turning up was unbeknown to us otherwise we would have called you as soon as we knew. Thanks, Student Cribs

reviews.text:
My son (in his first year) asked me to look at a student house online for him today as he was rushing off to a lecture and houses for next year are ‘flying off the shelves’.
Thus I came across Student Cribs, a company owning hundreds of student houses all over the country.
Their pitch is excellent. The houses look pristine, with double bedrooms, all mod cons and funky decor. Most appealingly, there is no need for a deposit and no fees upfront. This makes their high rents a little easier to stomach.
However, on calling their letting team, I was disturbed to find the following:
• All lets are for 52 weeks with no option of a shorter let, which seems unfair for a company dealing exclusively with students.
• Despite their claim online, they were reluctant to let me see a copy of their standard tenancy agreement, which is not released until each prospective tenant has paid an unrefundable £200 deposit. To be fair, I didn't push for this because by the time it came to the end of the phone call I knew I would never want to deal with this company.
• Bills are said to be included, yet there is a separate charge per person per week. In this case it was £17 (an average of £295 per month for the four-person household) for gas, electricity, water, TV licence, internet and end of tenancy clean. Bearing in mind that students are at university for perhaps 30 of the 52 weeks they’re paying for, these bills are very high. There is also a 'fair use' policy (which they decide upon), so if they decide you're using 'too much' electricity they can issue you with a higher charge.
• Since there is no deposit, tenants cannot simply lose part or all of their deposit if anything is lost or damaged. Instead, they are charged in full for anything this company seems necessary. Crucially, there is no financial limit for the tenants' liability and no third party (such as the Deposit Protection Scheme) to oversee what is fair and what isn’t. If they decide that the house needs recarpeting or redecorating throughout, or that the kitchen needs to be replaced, the tenants could be held responsible for the entire bill at the end of the tenancy.
Students are rushed through the letting decision with the frenzied feel of the January sales, 11 months before their rental is due to start, and I’m very concerned that these students, barely out of school, are being panicked into a really egregious contract.
Luckily my son and his friends pulled out, thinking that this company was ‘dodgy’. But there will be thousands of other students risking a huge bill at the end of their tenancy.
Think carefully about using them. If you are ultra clean, tidy and careful with your 'crib', and never want to invite any friends round for a few drinks, they may be fine for you. But I wouldn't want to spill anything on the carpet... Is this what you really want from your student accommodation?
If you do go ahead, make sure you have a really comprehensive home insurance policy, check the inventory very carefully, take photos of everything when you move in and document all of your contact with them (preferably by email).
reviews.reply_text:
Hi Kate,

Thank you for reviewing Student Cribs and I’m sorry that you feel you have had a bad experience. Communication is a priority of ours, but I think there may have been a miscommunication from a member of the lettings team in this instance. We are happy to share our sample tenancy agreement with potential tenants and guarantors and are planning on automatically issuing this as part of upcoming process changes. Our lettings team did not send you a sample tenancy agreement in this instance as we were in the midst of signing off this year’s tenancy agreement with our lawyers and would not have wanted to send you an incorrect agreement. This has now been signed off and It’d be great to send you the finalised tenancy agreement as it addressed points mentioned in your review:

• A holding retainer is industry standard within property rental. This is to cover the abortive administrative cost if a group decide not to take a property after advertising has stopped. Upon contracting this is returned to the group and contributes to their first month’s rent.
• Our fair usage policies are more than fair and have not been exceeded by a tenant to date. The usage is also averaged out for peak and off-peak usage further explanation on this is also included in the tenancy agreement.
• Fair wear and tear are standard for our tenancies and we do not charge tenants for this. However, we do have a schedule of charges within our tenancy agreement which are agreed to before tenants move in within the tenancy agreement, these costs are based upon average historic costs to repair or replace. We also provide third party inventories in between tenancies to document condition on check in and check out.

I am sorry in this situation for the miscommunication regarding our tenancy agreement. As always, we advise potential tenants and guarantors to read contracts full prior to signing and if they feel uncomfortable to seek independent advice from the universities housing office.

I have looked in our system and unfortunately can’t find anyone with your name as enquiring recently. It’d be great with the above points remedied if you would consider our services for the next academic year. If you are interested in more information, please email lettings@student-cribs.com or call 020 3758 7000 and a member of the lettings team will be happy to discuss further.

Thanks,
Student Cribs

reviews.text:
The house was lovely and well-equipped, especially for the reasonable rent (in Manchester): spacious rooms, well-equipped kitchen (a dishwasher, washing machine, tumble dryer and plenty of gas hobs), plenty of storage space,  as well as a nice little garden!

The sticking point was with maintenance: the biggest issue was that appliances took forever to be fixed. The dishwasher took weeks to be fixed and the tumble dryer was found to be one of those more likely to catch fire due to faulty parts (not Student Cribs' fault, to be clear), so we were instructed not to use it for several weeks... in a house of 5. The lack of communication and help from our property manager generally was infuriating to say the least; I understand that they wouldn't have just looked after our house, but it was disheartening and frustrating to get an incomplete answer to a question asked a week earlier.

On the whole the place was nice, the rent was affordable and the experience was tolerable, but the maintenance issues definitely  detracted from the year spent there. Your mileage may vary.

It has been great to live in a Student cribs house for the last year- the staff are very informative and are always willing to provide assistance if it is needed with the house. The maintenance team are always prompt and do their job to the highest degree, which is a rarity in a university house! I was amazed at the quality of the house when we moved in- very spacious bedrooms and a large kitchen and living room which has certainly made us the envy of our friends. We have enjoyed living here so much that we have chosen to continue living in a Student Cribs house for another year; I think this decision speaks for itself as a testament to this company and the quality of their company.

AMAZING! Couldn't fault student cribs. The house is spacious and well maintained, very quick response if anything was broken or faulty the maintenance man would come round either that day or the day after. The staff are very reasonable and understand student needs for example a bike rack was kindly put in our back garden in order for my housemate to secure his bike safely. There is excellent communication between tenants and staff which is greatly useful. They understand the house can get messy but give you plenty of warning for any visits and respect the tenants privacy! Honestly, an excellent company who pride themselves on being understanding and efficient. Highly Recommend!

I was initially skeptical about Student Cribs as the prices are a little higher than for other student houses in the area (Durham). However, it has turned out to be worth every penny. The only disappointment I have had is that it cost £50 to be given the code for the key lock box outside of the house when I was locked out! Despite this, not only is the house large with high quality and modern interiors, there is nothing we need to worry about with regards to maintenance. Our wonderful customer service manager Jessica sorts out any issues very promptly as soon as I email her. The moving in process was very simple (no need to worry about meter readings, organising utilities etc) and the bills inclusive option has turned out to be very good value for money (with an end of tenancy clean included.) I will be very sad to leave at the end of the year!
Highly recommended. Issues have always been taken care of and solved quickly, great service and place to stay!
Our house has been lovely this year - a very nice standard and very comfortable, will be sad to leave

Student Cribs have always been incredibly efficient and professional in handling clients, particularly as my house are all students and it was our first time renting. The house was of a pretty high standard, definitely nicer than the majority of student homes available. We also had a maintenance man on hand, Gary, who has been amazing at helping out and letting us know our responsibility as tenants! Overall a great first house, with just a few minor issues (such as a leaking shower) - however this did not detract from a brilliant experience with ththe company.

I lived in the peel street flat in Nottingham, it was very modern accommodation and had plenty of storage in the bedrooms. The kitchen space was very sociable and well equipped for students. Staff were always quick to respond if we had any issues and they made moving in a really simple process. Thank you student cribs!
We love our Student Cribs house! Really friendly and helpful staff, would definitely recommend.
