Ideal location in the centre of Fallowfield right by the bus stop, Sainsbury’s and Friendship. The sofas in our flat were absolutely horrendous but the rest of the kitchen furniture was decent. Slow WiFi, rubbish water pressure and loud residents standing outside their flats smoking and shouting till the early hours. All of the team are lovely (especially Will) apart from Ian who, to put it bluntly, isn’t very friendly (maybe he woke up on the wrong side of the bed those days?). All in all, 3/5, wouldn’t stay again.

Excellent accommodation! Although the property is on the higher end in terms of price, the location within the city is as good as you'll get.

The team are amazing and run the place well. Quality of furniture is excellent and the size of the properties are great. Underground car park is also a huge bonus! Would 100% percent recommend.
Nobody cares that somebody left pub in apartment and Nobody lets in no helpers

Sahara desert, outer space, the bottom of the Pacific ocean - 3 places where you will find a better internet connection than the one at Mayfair Court. The website advertises the WiFi as 'superfast' which is kinda mad. They also say they offer '50mbps speeds thoughout' but I'm pretty sure they meant to write 5 or its just false advertising. I can't watch football, play Ps4 online or use Netflix. Using the Internet for general searches isn't too bad though even that can be slow at times, but if you want to watch a YouTube video or something you're better off going home to watch it than trying to get it working here.
The rooms are decent sizes (in the 3 bed flat) and the wooden furniture is nice however the desk is so narrow you can barely fit an a4 paper on it so I just use it as a TV stand instead. Also bare in mind there's no locks on the doors. The beds massive but try not to lean back or the mattress will slide off. I think my phone torch would give more light to the room though cos the lighting is extremely dull. The en suite bathroom is good and easy to maintain but the locks tend to come off the bathroom doors and I've nearly got locked in my own bathroom a couple of times. Kitchen is massive and a nice open space can't really say anything negative about it except that when we first moved in turning the cooker/oven on would cause a blackout in the flat and it took 3 weeks for it to be replaced. Also, whenever you use the washing machine it feels like there's an earthquake but once you adjust to that sensation you'll be fine.
The area is alright cos there's lots of takeaways and shops around, but there's a also a brothel across the road so basically after 10pm there's all sorts of strange nittys walking about. Also there's lots of shouting going on throughout the night which will wake you up at times,and of it's not the people outside waking you up it's Mayfair courts fire alarm which seems to have a mind of its own. In terms of uni it's around a 50 minute work to mmu and 35/40 min walk to UoM so you may have to get a bus pass which is expensive.
Also be EXTREMELY careful with everything cos you will get charged with no hesitation if something messes up (probably why the deposit is so high at £250). To be honest I'd rather get normal student accommodation through uni like I did last year as they are a lot more understanding about things and issues with the accommodation get resolved with a whole lot more urgency. Mayfair Court emphasises that they are different from normal accommodation cos they offer more of an apartment style for young adults as opposed to typical student accommodation but all this means is your problems take 10x longer to be sorted but they're 10x quicker to charge you for anything that goes wrong, and some of the staff will wind you up with their sly comments. I understand some charges are fair but sometimes the staff feel like greedy landlords whereas I found with university accommodation last year they were much more empathetic to the tenants point of view.
If you do move here don't join the Facebook group cos all you'll see is loads of posts saying 'nobody likes your music turn it off' in 10,000 different ways, nothing useful.
Overall it definitely has good points but no way is it worth £145 a week in my opinion especially being so far from uni and the city centre. Also the contract ends at the end of July so if you're like me and your uni course finishes in May you'll be paying for nothing for a couple of months.
So in conclusion I would recommend moving here if you are out of options and homelessness is the only alternative. I was really excited to move here after the viewing but it has not lived up to expectations, I'm not biased in any way, I've just been frustrated with many things about this place and don't want other people to go through that too. I've gave 2 stars out of sympathy.

Pretty expensive for what it is. For reference, I stayed in a 2 bed, 2 bath apartment for £147.50 per week.

Post is well handled, the staff/RC team are all great, and it's a good location. Rooms are fairly spacious, but the desks are very narrow.

Furnishings are not modern apart from the sofas. The coffee tables etc are a bit 70s for a 'modern' appartment. The electric radiators are also pretty old and can let out an interesting smell.

Internet is advertised as 'up to' 100 Mbps, but you're lucky to get 10 during the evening. This is their internet provider's fault really, but still not really acceptable. There is also only WiFi available as far as I can tell, no Ethernet ports.

I've had leaks during heavy rainfall due to poor window seals, which has the added bonus of making soundproofing non existent. During a storm it caused large parts of the kitchen/living room area to flood (I'm not on the ground floor). I raised this the first time it happened and it was inspected, but nothing happened. I suspect because it requires more serious building/maintenance works.

Further to the sound proofing, I assume it's luck of the draw whether your flat will have decent window seals or not, but given that I don't and Cubo (the club) is right outside my bedroom window, be prepared for muffled but pretty loud music until 2am on the nights that they're open.

Floors/ceilings and walls are not soundproofed well at all, so you'll always be able to hear your upstairs neighbours.

On site secure parking is also extra, something to bear in mind if you drive.

The place is decent, I just wouldn't expect these problems for the price I'm paying. How much these would affect you depends on the room you get assigned.
Super rooms
Great looking place. Really smart looking and very well run. Recommended

Overall it has been a great experience staying at Mayfair Court. The location is great with everything you need close by. It’s a minute walk from the bus stop, there is a big Sainsbury’s right opposite and a Weatherspoons, McDonalds, Nando's and many more restaurants all within a five minute walk. There is also a pharmacy, hair dressers and the Owen’s Park computer cluster all near by. It is a very safe and secure accommodation with friendly and helpful staff. Parking space is also available. The flats are clean, modern and very spacious.

I have lived at this accommodation for 3 years now! I have nothing but great stuff to say. The staff have always treated us with care and always looked after us on a personal level even though there is loads of tenants they have to care for. The maintenance man Alan is always so happy to help and puts everybody first which has always been appreciated by me and my friends. The location is perfect lots of places to eat and drink that are always busy and full of friendly faces. There is also lots of shops including a huge sainsburys. It also never feels far from town when there is a bus stop on your door step that has buses into town nearly every 15 minutes. The flats have always been in great conditions every year, everything clean and new. Anyone that is thinking about staying here I would strongly recommend it. I have always felt at home and safe here which is a very important when you are in a new city finding your feet.

I have lived in a 3 bedroom apartment in Mayfair Court for three years. It has a nice modern feel with large rooms and an open plan living area. The building is very secure and in a fantastic location, everything you need is right on the doorstep (sainsbury's, bus route and pubs). The staff are all very friendly and helpful. When we moved in mezzino had recently taken over the building and it has improved a lot while we have been here.
Have been living there for two years with family in two bed rooms apartment. We love it. The location is great. The staffs are really friendly and helpful.
Lived here for two years now. The room is great, the staffs are very helpful, and the environment is very nice.

I have enjoyed living in Mayfair Court for the past year. The location is convenient with the bus stop right outside and being right across Sainsbury's. The place feels safe and the area is generally secure. It’s not perfect but I am super satisfied with the condition and furnishings of the flats, maintenance usually resolves my issues fairly quick. Highly recommend as a student accommodation!

Lived here for three years now and have experienced how the place has changed since mezzino bought the building. Vast improvement in the facilities and living areas. Staff respond quickly to any issues I have had and any maintenance work needed is sorted within a couple of days. Communal areas are cleaned daily in the week as is the car park and ramp. Couldn’t get a better location if you are a student, bus stop as soon as you walk out the door to get into university. Finally feels very secure and gated car parking is great. Only recommendation is to keep updating the flats, but besides that my experience has been 5 star!
A good place to live, great location and helpful/friendly staff 24/7.

Mayfair Court is a great place to live. Safe location, beautiful apartments and the staff is always very helpful. I've been living there for a year and I have already booked for next year. Would highly recomend!
Mayfair court is a really safe student accommodation with excellent staff that are very friendly and helpful. Stayed here this year and have already booked for next year. Would recommend to anyone!

I lived in Chancellors Court which is modern, spacious and secure. It is nearby to Sainsbury’s, pharmacy and a local chip shop. The rooms have everything you need and the bathroom has bath. The service is always friendly and helpful.

Best place to be interms of saftey and location!! The team of staff members are really helpful, as well as the onsite members who are useful for fixing any problems you've got! Second year returning, don't miss out guys!!
They charge you such a high rent, but when you move in, don’t expect the flat to have been cleaned, and expect everything to fall apart without ANY help from maintenance

Mayfair Court is in the heart of Fallowfield , with a direct bus route to the centre of Manchester, what more could you want! Within the Mezzino team they have friendly staff who assist with any possible needs for your flat. Overall it’s a lovely place to live and I’m looking forward to a second year at Mayfair Court.
Great place to live. Great location, well furnished and a well ran place. Already signed up to stay again next year!

Currrntly staying here for my 3rd year of university and highly considering staying here again as a postgrad/professional. Super happy with the condition and furnishings in the flat and any maintenance issues have been resolved quickly. Site manager Ian and assistant Will are always happy to help. Location is great for bus access to uni and town centre. Would highly recommend

WORST ACCOMMODATION EVER. Rent is incredibly high for a place where YOU CANNOT EVEN USE YOUR OWN SHOWER. Try ask for maintenance? Your priorities are at the bottom of their agenda. They advertise this place to be a "modern" accommodation, when all the appliances are incredibly outdated. The appliances make weird loud noises all night too, which will disrupt your sleep on the regular. "All bills included" ? That's because the shower doesn't even get hot,  And if it does get hot, be ready for it to suddenly stop in the middle of your shower, or turn burning hot or freezing cold, and if you try complain, they say the rest of the building faces the same issue. Ridiculous. Worst housing experience ever.
Not sure if it's because it's on the scruffiest road in Manchester but my apartment was infested with rats.

For the price that you pay they take too long to fix any issues you have.  The electrics in there  seem outdated. As a member of staff told me that the have had to disconnect some of the heaters in the flats because it cuts off the electrisaty.  Spoke to another tenent in there aparantly when they moved in there was mice/rat droppings

You'll be spending a pretty penny on a nice view, if you're lucky enough to be on the right side. Prepare for half hour showers, ducking in when the heating finally decides to kick in and jumping out when it shoots back to freezing, and that was after our shower was replaced. They have a helpful 24hr service to let you in when you're locked out which is nice. However maintainence was never their highest priority, you'll have to pester them half the time to get a job done. It's a cheaply built building so expect the cheap plastic to fall off and even your door to stop working occasionally. Bills included is nice, but intermittent WiFi is less pleasant. Dont expect any help from them, even as simple as opening the car park to make moving out an easy transition. You can expect to hear every movement in the flat above! This place was quite infuriating to live in, but being next to the bus stop, Sainsburys and our view across Manchester, it wasn't the worst housing experience in Manchester.

Stayed here for two years so far and staying with them for another next year. Staff are super friendly and helpful. The maintenance team are very helpful and only ever need things fixing once on the rare occasions they are needed. Perfect location too!
Friendly staff, perfect location, no big complaints and I'm very happy to live here as a student.
Great modern student apartments! Efficient maintenance staff! Amazing members of staff, who are always there when you need them :)

Moving out in a month and after 10 months of complaints, over 40 maintenance issues reported.
A Christmas that was freezing cold due to promises about fixing windows that was never met.
Would not recommend this place to anyone.
After 10 months of being ignored and passed from one person to the next I can officially say it was the worst accommodation I have stayed at during my time at uni.
Sam came to fix our door quick and rapid service :)

Sam from the maintenance team at Student Cribs was incredibly helpful. He was already at the house carrying out a different job when I mentioned that some of the drawers were jammed. He immediately took the time to come and have a look, which I really appreciated. He was friendly, professional, and went out of his way to help. Excellent service and a credit to the maintenance team.

Had a maintenance issue with our 2 bedroom flat, reported on the weekend, and they were very quick to respond and the following Monday someone, Called Sam, came over to fix it. Didn’t even take more than 10 minutes to fix and was already done. Really appreciate the quick response to fix the issue
The maintenance team is hit and miss but we had our main door fixed and my blinds fixed by Sam who was very polite and he got the jobs done very quickly and didn’t disturb me or my flatmate at all.
Sam and Chris were incredible and helped us with a new fridge/freezer instillation. Thankyou so much!
Massive shout out to Sam from the maintenance team for fixing so many issues in our flat and being super reliable💪

Worst flat I have lived at in my 3 years at university.
Every flat is full of mould, cold and damp.
Issues take months to be sorted and nothing is taken serious.
£700 a month for a room.
Awful.
Would not recommend and students should find a better place to live than here, overpriced and not taken care of.
Not to mention the 0 privacy.

My experience living at Mezzino during university was nothing short of a nightmare. From the start, the place was plagued by issues that significantly impacted my daily life and academic performance.

Firstly, we faced constant gas leaks, which created an unsettling atmosphere and raised safety concerns. The boiler was unreliable, leaving us without hot water for extended periods. Adding to this, the broken toilet seat was never repaired throughout the year, a clear indication of the lack of maintenance.

The state of the accommodation was also alarming. The carpets were stained from past rodent infestations, which left a lingering feeling of discomfort. To make matters worse, we had to evacuate the building four times during exam season due to safety concerns, severely disrupting my focus and ultimately affecting my final results.

Despite these numerous issues, the management failed to return our full deposit, which felt unjust given the poor living conditions we endured.

While Mezzino may boast appealing marketing, my experience leads me to believe it’s misleading. I would only recommend this place to those without significant responsibilities or concerns. For anyone serious about their studies and well-being, I suggest looking elsewhere.

Don't share sensitive
reviews.reply_text: Hello, I am sorry to hear about the issues you experienced whilst staying with us. Id be interested to get more information regarding your stay and would love for you to reach out to me directly via email at Alex@Mezzino.co.uk. I am part of the new management team here at Mezzino Manchester and I am very keen to hear about previous failings and how we can improve from them in future. I look forward to hearing from you.

reviews.text:
My daughter has been paying to live at this awful place since early September and it is disgusting. There is an extremely hazardous to health vermin problem which hasn't been dealt with and which began the day after she moved in, the place is crawling with silverfish (in my daughter's clothing and in all the rooms and corridors in the flat) - also not being dealt with, there is damp in the bedroom ceiling which has begun to fall down, there was no hot water when she moved in for threee days, the internal spiral staircase is falling apart, there's a living room window which isn't attached properly and looks about to fall off, the water was turned off for almost twenty-four hours with no prior warning nor any water offered as a replacement, oh, and none of the things mentioned here are classed as an emergency according to the tenants who are manning the 'emergency' phone line.
These things, my daughter is told, need logging on the student hub where they appear to be largely ignored and are not resolved. Management do not appear to want to know.
What a miserable and expensive time these young people are having at this place.
I have heard that positive posts are paid ones via gift card. They seldom have many likes either, whereas the negative posts get many likes. Wonder why that is.
reviews.reply_text: Hi Victoria, thank you for your feedback and we are sorry your daughter is experiencing these issues. Here at Mezzino Manchester we take all maintenance issues very serious and look to resolve them as quickly as possible in line with the our completion guidelines. In regards to the pest infestation, I would like to assure you that all reported issues are dealt with as soon as possible and this is always the case. I can also assure you that the management team here at Mezzino Manchester are always here to listen to tenants and help resolve issues where possible as soon as we can. If you could be so kind as to ask your daughter to come to the office to speak with us, we would be more than happy to discuss this matter and resolve any outstanding works that needing doing at this time. Alternatively if you would like to discuss your feedback further please feel free to email me at Alex@Mezzino.co.uk.

reviews.text: WARNING do not live here!!!! I showed up to my accomodation and it was absolutely disgusting, my door was kicked in, there was furniture in the middle of the floor because there was no room for it in the room, and lastly the doors have NO LOCK! Completely unsafe for any student and appalling accomodation. Staff are awful and don't reply to your emails. False marketing and advertisement with the pictures online.
reviews.reply_text:
Thank you for taking the time to share your feedback. We appreciate your insights as they help us enhance the experience for all our residents here at Mezzino Manchester.

Firstly, we extend our sincere apologies for the inconvenience you experienced with the readiness of your apartment upon check-in. Please be assured that we are actively working to ensure that such situations are avoided in the future. All our apartments are equipped with fully functional locks on the front doors and fob entry systems for every individual building to ensure security and convenience.

Additionally, our maintenance team are readily available on-site to promptly address any maintenance issues that may arise. 

Furthermore, we want to express that we are a new staff team here at Mezzino Manchester, and your feedback is invaluable in helping us improve. We would be delighted to meet with you personally to discuss your experience in more detail and gain further insights into areas where we can enhance our services. Please feel free to visit our office at Mayfair Court anytime Monday to Friday between 9 am and 5 pm.

reviews.text: When I signed the contract, I didn't realise I'd be sharing an apartment with black mould and silverfish. Still don't understand why Ian wore those toe shoes 🤮🦶
reviews.reply_text: Thank you for your review, we strive to take on any feedback constructively. After reviewing our internal system I have been unable to find any record of you as a tenant. Please could you contact us by emailing manchester@mezzino.co.uk with some more information, including which apartment you stayed in/are staying in.
Thank you for your positive review. We are happy to hear you have had a good experience within our accommodation! 😊

reviews.text: My friend and I have just finished out tenancy at Chancellors court, our flat was lovely and we are so sad to leave! The minimal issues we had were resolved straight away, so would definitely recommend Mezzino to students who are flat hunting.
reviews.reply_text: Thank you for your positive review. We are happy to hear you have had a good experience within our accommodation! 😊

reviews.text: My room mate and I have just spent the last 10 months in this student accommodation and have no bad things to say. The team are lovely and our flat , even though we booked late, was lovely. Throughout our tenancy , we have had no major issues and found the overall experience great and are very sad to leave. Would definitely recommend for this staying in Fallowfield area.
reviews.reply_text: Thank you for your positive review. We are happy to hear you have had a good experience within our accommodation! 😊

reviews.text: Carbon monoxide leaks on a regular basis, didn't get any money back despite having to sleep on the floor outside our flat
reviews.reply_text: Thank you for your review, we strive to take on any feedback constructively. Feel free to contact us via email (manchester@mezzino.co.uk) and let us know how we could have dealt with your issues better.
Thank you for your positive review. We are happy to hear you have had a good experience within our accommodation! 😊
Thank you for your positive review. We are happy to hear you have had a good experience within our accommodation! 😊

reviews.text: Great location and lovely staff!
reviews.reply_text: Thank you for your positive review. We are happy to hear you have had a good experience within our accommodation! 😊

reviews.text:
Absolutely disgusting lived here in my third year at university and was shocked and appalled. There was water damage everywhere. The bath leaked onto the fuze box multiple times, whenever mice were found it’d take them months to even respond to anything. When I finally left they did not give my deposit back and through months of trying I’ve now had to give up on getting my deposit back and they refuse to give me the number to head office.

No matter how late you leave it getting a flat or if you even think it looks good, AVOID. AVOID LIKE THE PLAGUE.
The accommodation itself it lovely very much a more apartment/flat feel more than student accommodation, problems are fixed as soon as they are noticed, shops near by and bus stop too.
reviews.reply_text: Thank you for your positive review. We are happy to hear you have had a good experience within our accommodation! 😊

reviews.text: My daughter stayed at Chancellors court for 2 years brilliant accommodation her and flatmate really enjoyed it location perfect also. Dave.
reviews.reply_text: Thank you for your positive review. We are happy to hear your daughter had a good experience within our accommodation! 😊

reviews.text: Stayed her for two years, not had many issues and issues I’ve had have been resolved quickly, enjoyed my stay and would recommend!
reviews.reply_text: Thank you for your positive review. We are happy to hear you have had a good experience within our accommodation! 😊

reviews.text:
Lived here in my first year of university.
The amount of issues was ridiculous.
1. Maintenance issues on arrival that were never fixed despite requests: just a few of the problems: broken dining room furnite, cigarette smell in lounge, broken hob, no lock on doors, unpainted ceiling with plaster patches, disgusting desk chair ripped material completely, no toilet roll holder.
2. Maintenance throughout the year would either not fix the issues or come and make a massive mess and leave it all over bathroom floor, bedroom floor and bed.
3. The flat i lived in compared to other friends flats I saw were completely different. mine was totally unrefurbished, in need of a deep clean and many broken things, but when you apply for a room you have a chance of being given this room that is the opposite of the nice new ones advertised on the website and at showings.
4. there was so many silverfish, spiders, bugs and all sorts throughout the whole year
5. they tried to refuse our deposit refund due to a broken table that was broken when we moved in and noted in the inventory checklist
6. when i complained about my overall experience they said they would refund my deposit (as mentioned above) in exchange for no bad reviews on social media.
7. the bathroom deserves its own point as it was in such horrible condition- halfway through the year they made a very large shoebox sized shape hole in the ceiling above the shower for maintenance and never covered it up and so we had so many bugs and spiders coming straight down into the shower because of this.

Overall I would not recommending paying the price for this accommodation unless they show you which exact flat you will be getting so you cant be surprised with a horrible one as i was, otherwise don't waste your time or money here there are so many more affordable and satisfactory places than here where even the customer service was terrible.
Just search Mezzino on MSG and think twice

ABSOLUTLEY appalling that this accommodation is still going. I lived here from September 2021 with 4 other people. The place was riddled with silverfish. We had rodents running across our kitchen, in our cupboards and also in the living room. At one point, our washing machine wire started sparking and burnt a hole through the plug, we reported it. HEARD NOTHING for weeks!!! The pictures that are shown are a lie, rooms are smaller with broken furniture holes in the walls and broken toilet seats. I asked to come and visit this place before I put a deposit down. I got told no numerous times due to the flats needed more furniture and cleaning. However the first day I moved in, there was mold in the bathroom everywhere. We had no hot water for days - ignored us again. Would not let my own dogs live there. AVOID LIKE THE PLAUGE

None of my flats maintenance issues get sorted, emails regarding issues also get point blank ignored. Maintenance problems that are a must, including a shower rail, without it, we are left in a pool of water, after each use and the mattress feels like I’m sleeping on a pile of bullets.

Living here. It’s absolutely terrible, it’s been about 3 weeks since we lodged the maintenance report, and nothing has changed even though we are literally living in a health and safety violation. In addition to this, I’m paying 6.5 grand to share a room with literal rodents, you can hear them crawling and squeaking in the walls. all the good reviews were paid off with Amazon gift cards 0/10 wouldn’t reccomend to my complete worst enemy.

SHOCKING ACCOMMODATION. Absolute scammers. DO NOT BE FOOLED BY PICTURES OR GOOD REVIEWS. I moved in three weeks ago expecting the place to be sorted and I had to sign an inventory form to say what was missing or broken. Three weeks have passed and nothing has been sorted. I currently have broken furniture in my room which they said they would replace two weeks ago. There is also a serious mice problem which they know about but have not made any endeavour to sort. Some of the carpets are stained red. Some of the lights don’t have covers. The staircase is broken in places. I have logged 8 maintenance issues on their system and none have been attended to.
Great accommodation and lovely staff.
Nice but need bit more focus on cleaning...

Mice everywhere, don’t be fooled by the pictures, literally one of the worst accommodations. Came in and everything was dirty, literally had mushrooms growing under the bed flooring at one point. Definitely would not recommend.

I’ve been at chancellors for about a year now and I’ve loved it! Location, friendly staff with good opportunities for students!

The flats are very homely and of good standard with a few imperfections but nothing at all to truly complain about!

If you’re a student looking to move to Fallowfield I’d recommend it everytime! Me and my flat mate are staying on for another year!

Terrible! My boyfriend's flat had no heating for the majority of the time he lived there. There was mould in the bathroom. None of the maintenance complaints were adequately followed up. They're also incredibly disorganized and haven't played the bills (of which they were contractually obligated to) on occasion. DO NOT MOVE HERE!
Perfect location for students and our flat was a great size for two people. Super friendly/helpful staff who always respond quickly to any queries/issues - will be booking again for next year!
Great location and easy access. Perfect for students.
ideal location, friendly staff, maintenance issues fixed promptly - overall a great flat and good place to live
Covid jabs done xx

The fact I have lived in, and indeed returned to, Chancellors Court for 3 years now, summarises my deeply rooted fondness for the apartment complex. It is perfect for students, with the rooms and living areas being spacious, comfortable and homely. However, it is the staff who really make Chancellors Court an enjoyable place to live. Whether it be Alan's unending smile, or Ian's cheerful welcome, it is Mezzino's everyday faces which make it special. I have had the best 3 years here and I would highly recommend it to any student. 10/10.

I lived here during my third year at uni I was impressed by how quick and easy it was to acquire a flat here. Chancellors Court is a great location situated on Moseley Road in Fallowfield with lots of pubs, bars and shops to suit all needs. The staff are very professional and friendly, dealing with any issues swiftly. The rooms are all of good size as well as offering a spacious living room and kitchen. I would reccomend this accomodation to anyone who would like to move to the Fallowfield area .

Stayed here for my 2nd and 3rd year of university and I have had no problems with them. The staff are helpful, location is perfect for student life and the compound is very safe, unlike certain areas of Manchester. Would strongly recommend.
Rough area

Lived in chancellors for 2020 and the beginning of 2021, the flat is spacious and the rooms are the perfect size for a student. You a gated parking are, which is ideal for students bringing their car to Manchester, it also adds an extra layer of security. I am very pleased with Mezzino.

Lived in Chancellors Court for 3 years now after coming out of halls. The apartments are modern and spacious with all bills included, the carpark is gated with key fob access which is perfect if you are planning on bringing your car to uni. The maintenance team are always quick to resolve any issues within the flat and the team in the office are very approachable.

If you're looking for somewhere in Fallowfield that is a level up from the usual student housing I'd highly recommend Mezzino.
COVID NEEDLE
Great location and any problems swiftly dealt with in a good manner by Ian and Melissa in office who are great! Would recommend to any student :)
Few minor problems but dealt with professionally and swiftly by staff. Great living experience in a great student location in Fallowfield.

Over the course of the January period, we experienced a severe downpour, especially in Manchester. This caused a flood in my apartment due to a heavy leakage from the boiler overflowing with water. This resulted in the boiler having to be switched off, meaning no hot water or heating. This coincided with it snowing as well so I was quite distressed. However, the Mezzino team provided me with numerous heaters as well as hot water facilities and managed to resolve the issue in timely fashion. The rapid response to the situation meant that the boiler was up and running with a matter of days. The team handled the situation excellently, especially due to the impact of Covid-19. Thank you to Ian and the team as without you guys, I would still be without a functioning boiler in the middle of winter!
Gorgeous flat, beautiful and well situated
Excellent accommodation for university students, spacious room sizing and good security, would highly recommend.

First year living in Chancellors Court and it is great value for money. Very nice modern flats, feel safe and secure around the area and it is a few minutes walk to all bus stops, Sainsbury’s, nandos and many other shops. Would definitely recommend!
Great sized rooms with new appliances and if there was to be any issues they are resolved very quickly.
Great student accommodation in a good area, visited friends here multiple times and i'm very impressed with the facilities. Definitely considering for next year.
Fantastic apartments, lovely area, staff super friendly and on hand to deal with an issues 24/7. 5/5
Lovely staff, Amazing view, affordable - KB

After living here for just under three months, comparing this to the halls I had last year, chancellors court is by far the better accommodation. The Kitchen, rooms and living area are of a good size. The maintenance team are always helpful when fixing occasional things that go wrong, for example I needed a curtain rail put up in my room as it got too light in the mornings, this was done within the week I asked. It does have small problems but everywhere else we looked was the same.
Brilliant accommodation in a great area for students, staff are always friendly and I'm certainly rebooking for next year.
Great accommodation, with good sized bedrooms, in a good student area. There is also easy access to a variety of to public transport routes and shops.
Clean well appointed student halls

I can't emphasise enough how bad my time at Chancellor's Court was from the dirty apartment with broken appliances and facilities to the shocking attitude of the staff. We were showed a example apartment on our visit which was quite nice and clean but clearly this wasn't the standard of all apartments since ours was much darker and incredibly dirty when we moved in. I soon realised that the oven wouldn't work and the toilet wouldn't flush; i told the office staff about the issues and it took almost two months for us to get a new oven so our cooking was very limited and the toilet was never properly fixed the whole time that I was there! I quickly decided that I would rather move back home and commute to university but when I made this clear to the staff, they were extremely unhelpful and were actually quite rude. I eventually found someone to take over my tenancy but they rejected this person for some unknown reason which they refused to talk to me about leaving me with no choice but to continue paying the rent but moving back home anyway so I wasted a lot of money but was prepared to do so because the apartment was that bad and the staff only cared about receiving rent and not the welfare of their tenants! Any time we tried to come to an agreement to at least reduce my rent they just threatened charging us interest and other charges.

The mezzino staff are very warm and welcoming and if there ever was a problem with the flat they made sure that the issue was resolved quickly. The flat we stayed in was good and I’ve enjoyed my year living in Fallowfield with Mezzino.

I have been living in this accommodation for a year now (renewing it for 2020) and I have not had issues with it. Reading the negative reviews and all I can say is don't listen to them. Whenever I had a maintenance issue it would get sorted on the same day or first thing in the morning. Not once has the office been rude or unprofessional, they have been very good with all the tenants and dealt with any issues straight away. This student accommodation is really good and affordable, with safe parking and CCTV across all buildings. Bills and WIFI all included so never have to worry about that. Yes, there sometimes the gates stopped working but it gets fixed on the same day. Just come view the properties and see how you feel rather than listening to the negative reviews as from what I can see they are all lies, if they were true I wouldn't be renewing the tenancy for next year. Because it sounds to me that those negative reviews are the people that either never lived in any of the properties or they didn't allow the maintenance guy to fix their issue and looking for attention.

We unfortunately used Mezzino for many committee meetings. The accommodation is one of the worst I have seen in Manchester. My colleague also agrees. They told me of very poor service by the management and bad internet. There was also a distinct lack of security at the site.

Firstly, I would like to advise anyone reading these reviews to do so with caution because Mezzino pay tenants to post positive reviews. This is typical of the unethical practices of Mezzino as a company generally.

My flatmates and I have had an extremely poor experience living at Mezzino, initially we moved into a flat which had not been cleaned and had several glaring maintenance issues. Upon complaining about these issues we were assured they would be resolved. These issues were never fully seen to and any attempts to speak to Mezzino staff about timelines for getting to the issues were met with excuses.

Mezzino have provided poor maintenance all year, for example, when our washing machine broke we went 5 weeks without any convenient washing facilities.

In general I would not suggest living here for the reasons mentioned above, furthermore the accommodation manager is very hostile and condescending and does not take well to tenants wanting issues in their flat resolved.

We had the distinct displeasure of using Mezzino for committee meetings, I can wholeheartedly say that this is one of the worst accommodations in Manchester. My colleague informs me that the staff are rude and dismissive. Tenants are also paid to write positive reviews. Hugely unethical behaviour and not Hindu Society approved.

And also, the WiFi is awful.

Paying people to give them 5 stars on here rather than actually working for it. Terrible service, terrible conduct and an accommodation manager with a condescending and rude attitude, my list of grievances go on and on. AVOID at all costs

Terrible place to live, apartment was riddled with countless issues when we moved in. In addition to that, the apartment was not cleaned. The staff are very evasive if you try to report any issues, I would not recommend this place to anyone.

And also, the WiFi is terrible.

I have lived in Chancellors Court for the past year and have had a pleasant experience with no major issues, and any issues I did have were resolved quickly and efficiently. The flat is a good size, with an especially big kitchen which is nice. Great location in the centre of fallowfield, near to Sainsbury’s and the bus stop. Would gladly stay here again if I wasn’t graduating, would recommend.

ATTENTION, do not believe the 5 star ratings, numerous sources have admitted that they have been paying people £20/30 vouchers to give them 5 star ratings and bring their online rating up, dodgy, unethical business! Categorically the worst place I have stayed in Manchester, as a 3rd year, my expectations with respect to landlords were pretty okay, but after this experience I will never trust another landlord, the initial squalid living conditions when we moved in, coupled with the lack of professional conduct and general safety of the flat with no room keys, and consistently broken gates, if you’re thinking of staying here, do yourselves a favour and don’t.
Safe and affordable housing with very good sized rooms. Flats are great quality and maintenance responds very quickly. Would definitely recommend to students looking for safe housing on a budget.
Good sized rooms, nice furniture, and staff are very nice and helpful. Would recommend to any student!

I have lived in Chancellors Court, Manchester for the past 2 years in two different flats. It is a very clean and well maintained accommodation with really helpful staff that are on hand to help anytime you need. All of the facilities that I have used have worked extremely well and I have never had any major issues. Room sizes are great, with lots of furniture provided for storage. It is very secure with fob entry at multiple doors before you get to your own flat door. Its very well located with a big supermarket on the same road and the bus route at the end of the road. The staff are happy to accept deliveries on your behalf at their office so there is no worry about missing a delivery.
Good sized modern flats, fair utility cap and helpful staff and office members 👍🏻
Great accommodation with friendly staff and spacious, nicely refurbished rooms! Also, the location is great for student life!

Highly recommend, been here 2 years and signed up for my third. Flats are clean, welcoming and  warm. Double glazing & heating is a rare feature in this price bracket. Well furnished but are very happy to remove items for you if you prefer your own. Repairs are always carried out within a few days of you reporting it and staff are extremely friendly. Not looking forward to moving out.

Lived here for the last year, very friendly staff who were flexible when booking as I was in America at the time. Flat is in a great location, close to everything, good size, and everything was clean and up to a good standard of living, any complaints were dealt with quickly. Couldn't fault it, I'd live here again if I wasn't graduating this year!

Great accommodation, ideal for students as its close to bus stops and shops as well as pubs and restaurants. The staff are all lovely and so helpful. The apartments are clean, spacious and well kept. Any maintenance issues reported are dealt with within a week tops. I've re-booked for another year. Definitely recommend. :)
Stayed here for 2 years, highly recommend! Good team of staff and maintence. The accommodation is safe and secure. Ideal location for students with flats being a good size.

Lived here 2 full years and never had a problem. Any maintenance issues have been resolved on the day if not the next. Internets fast and reliable. Great location, not far from main bus route, supermarket, bars and takeaways. Room sizes are decent and comfortable with all the main furniture and appliances you'd expect. Office staff are friendly. Fob entry so it feels more secure. Recommended some friends to live here as well and they like it too.

Stayed here for 2 years, newly refurbished flats with plenty of space was key to making me want to rebook, Along with the benefits of being in a safe and ideal location for student life with supermarkets, pubs and restaurants all within 2 minutes away.
Highly Recommend.

Great accommodation to stay at and location. Already re-booked for next year as I have had no issues with the flat or staff. The staff is friendly and helpful, and whenever something needs fixing around the flat it gets sorted within 24 hours. Would definitely recommend!
Good team, People who work there are very helpful but accommodation is lacking. Not worth the money you pay
Great location, good-sized bedrooms, very nice living room and kitchen areas. The staff are very helpful and friendly too. I would definitely recommend this place to students!

been staying here for 2 years - the place is clean and homey.
bus stops are near by and lots of places to eat. staff is really friendly and always ready to help with any problem you have. :)

I would highly recommend Chancellors court in Manchester to any students looking for accommodation, it’s in a great location near a big Sainsbury’s ,all bus stops going to universities/city centre and near all bars and restaurants in Fallowfield. Apartments are all very modern and spacious and come in a gated community with key fob access making it very secure unlike some student houses in the area. Staff are very friendly and are available pretty much all the time, especially the maintenance guy Alan , an absolute top bloke, he cannot do enough for you and aims to get any problems dealt with as soon as possible.Never had any major issues living here and have done so for 3 years.
Would definitely recommend. Been staying here for two years now. Everything is provided that you would possibly need. It's been an amazing experience.

Well maintained, spacious accomodation. Secure and in a great location near bus routes passing through Oxford road. Ian and Will are very helpful and quick to respond to any issues. Re-booking for next year without a doubt!

Definitely being undercharged for the accommodation. Regular maintanence is carried out and I have never had a problem with anything not to mention great location . Definitely rebooking for next academic year!

Never had a problem staying here. Good facilities to keep my car, and was able to get a ground floor flat to help with my dodgy leg. Internet is really good and solid, not drafts either as the heating is well behaved. Maintenance is usually very fast so all in all a nice place. I'll be sad to leave

Chancellors court to me is a good value for money place to live in a popular location among students.
As anyone could expect a few minor issues have risen here and there along my year stay, but all of these issues were resolved quickly once notified.
The staff have always been friendly and helpful in every instance I have been in contact with them with a multitude of things; personally I have found this to be very beneficial during my stay.
I would recommend Chancellors Court to anyone looking for a good value for money place to stay in Fallowfield.
Rooms are spacious and the kitchen is fully equipped. Maintenance guys are quick fixing any issues. Totally recommend.

I've lived here for 2 years now and I havent got a bad thing to say really. Ian and Will are really helpful and good lads who will help as much as they can. Alan the maintenance guy is also really helpul and he will get things sorted as soon as he can. All in all I've had an extremely pleasant stay here and ill be sad to leave.
Living here for 2nd year and loving it. Good sized, refurbished rooms and nice living room and kitchen. Would recommend it
Clean, Helpful staff, Great location, and fast action from maintence
Brilliant place to live as the apartments are spacious and furnished to a high standard, already signed to live here again next year!
Really good place to stay. Rooms are a good size and well furnished. Staff are really attentive, too. Will definitely be staying at Chancellors Court next year.
Really friendly staff, loads of space, feels less like an accommodation and more like a flat. Enjoyed staying here this year. Re booked for next year as-well.

Stayed here during 2nd year and it was great.
Staff are friendly, location is perfect for uni access and local amenities are close. Room size is generous and the flat was furnished well. Added security from fob access which is admirable. Would highly recommend!!

I have lived here for two years and would have happily stayed again if I wasn't graduating. Perfect accommodation for the more mature students as the design is more young professional than student giving it a more homely feel. I always felt safe and secure in this accommodation due to multiple doors and locks to get into each building/apartment/room. Every issue has been dealt with in a timely manner (such as light bulb replacements and even a whole boiler replacement was done ASAP and staff communicated this clearly with us).
Office staff have always been friendly and helpful. I couldn't recommend Chancellors Court enough!
Everything was broken never got my deposit back no safety at all in this building as fire alarms don't work!

Worst accommodation I have ever stayed in. Even the stairs are almost falling apart at the seams, only live here if you want no social life. On top of this the common room area is awfully designed and very small, while there is no general social site for people to commemorate. Avoid at all costs.

Do not reccomend and will not be re-signing next year.
I’ve listed just a fraction of the issues I’ve had in the past 5 months of living here:
1) Letters from debt companies thretaning to break into the flat and cut off heating due to outstanding debt - Mezzino still haven’t resolved issue.
2) TV license officer visiting the flat multiple times as the flat did not have a TV licence (despite this being in the contact) - took Mezzino 2 months to deal with issue
3) Rooms are not cleaned in between tenants (eg. Food spillages, gum in carpets when I moved in - reported and not dealt with)
4) Major appliances broken and not dealt with within time frames they specify
7) Rooms vary a great deal in size. Everyone pays the same but it’s down to luck whether you get a big/small bedroom.

I don't even live here, I just have been completely put off when viewing and I want to stop anyone from rushing in thinking they've found this perfect, cheap(ish) accommodation based on misleading images online.

I found out about student cribs several days ago, and was excited that they offered dual occupancy flats for my boyfriend and I to share. Initially I booked 3 remote viewings, as I live two hours away and didn't think I'd be able to make the time to go in person to view them. When it came to the time that my first viewing was supposed to happen, I received no call. I waited for 6 minutes, and then messaged a member of their staff in WhatsApp asking what's happening. I waited for another 20 minutes with no response from anyone, and sent the staff another WhatsApp message.

Finally, 35 minutes after my first viewing was supposed to start, I received a message back from the staff saying that the brand manager had apparently tried and failed to contact me (I received no calls or messages). At the same time, I received a message from the person who was supposed to be conducting my viewings, saying he had recorded videos of the flats. The remote viewings were advertised as being a real-time video call, but when I asked to video call, I was repeatedly told that he had already recorded videos for me.

When I was sent these videos, they were all a minute long and terribly recorded, with such fast switching between areas of rooms that i could barely tell what was going on. On top of this, I had previously received videos of a handful of flats from the staff member on WhatsApp (recorded in 2020), and I noticed that one of the flat viewings I was sent by one person was not that same flat is in the other video. At this point, I had decided to go see the flats in person. I was ignoring the red flags and hoping that it was just one bad viewings assistant, and that if I saw the flats in person everything would be fine.

Come today, I went to see the flats in person with my partner. The first flat was quite rough around the edges, and not in the way you would want. The appliances looked old, paint was peeling, stained, and mouldy in some places. It really was not what you would want for £200~ a week, NOT including gas and electrics.

Then we went to go try and see the second flat, and the brand manager was unable to get us into the building, and so we were taken to another flat first. This other flat was dark, the paintwork was terrible, and most worryingly, on the floor outside there was an incident notice from the fire department saying that electrics had been cut off due to an upstairs leak. This notice was from 2020, which was 6 years ago. ??? No one was currently living in this flat, so are the electrics still cut off? Why is the notice STILL there? So many red flags.

Then, we went back to try and view the other flats, and the brand manager left us to attempt to get some keys that might open up the front door. Once she came back, none of the keys worked, and so we literally just could not view the flats. At this point, I was completely put off by the flats and the company as a whole.

I also have read through the sample contracts on their website, and they are quite predatory. There's no promise that they will fix any broken white goods, so if maintenance is crap and your fridge is broken, sucks to suck. Also, nowhere does it say that the property will be cleaned before you move in. Furthermore, you won't be given your tenancy contract until you've paid a £200 holding retainer, which will not be refunded if you read the contract and then decide that you don't want to accept it. This is really, really predatory behaviour considering that this is a company that lets to students, who are known to struggle with money.

Overall, I've had a pretty terrible experience with student cribs, and I cannot recommend interacting with them at all. I'm so glad I didn't jump the gun and book anything.
reviews.reply_text:
Hi Estella,
 
Thank you for taking the time to leave your feedback. We’re really sorry to hear that your viewing experience did not reflect the standard of service we aim to provide.
 
Our usual process is for our Brand Managers to confirm attendance and details ahead of viewings. While this typically runs smoothly, occasional issues such as contact or device problems can interfere. We’ll be feeding this back into our ongoing training to ensure both in-person and virtual viewings are delivered clearly and professionally, including the quality of virtual tours.
 
The vacant flat you viewed became empty following a tenant dropout. While there have been no reports of an active leak at the property, we understand how the presence of external post and the overall presentation may have raised questions. We will be checking this internally to ensure the property is presented appropriately and that any outstanding housekeeping matters are addressed.
 
We have an in-house maintenance team and a reporting system in place for any issues raised by tenants. Properties are also inspected periodically, and professional cleans are carried out ahead of new tenancies. Safety-related works, including electrics, are handled in line with regulations, and we will be reviewing why signage referencing past works remained visible, as we appreciate that this can cause concern.
 
Regarding access to one of the flats, there are occasional situations where keys are not immediately available due to tenant use or access restrictions within buildings. That said, we appreciate that travelling to a viewing and being unable to gain entry is frustrating, and we will be reviewing how this was handled to minimise the risk of this happening again.
 
For clarity, the £200 holding retainer is refundable should an applicant decide not to proceed before completing the agreement, and if the tenancy goes ahead, it is deducted from the first rent payment. Our agreements also outline our responsibilities regarding maintenance and repairs.
 
We appreciate you sharing your experience, as feedback like this allows us to review our processes and make improvements where needed. If you would like to discuss anything further, please feel free to contact us directly.
 
Thank you, 
The Student Cribs Team

Boiler has been broken the whole of my stay
Easy and smooth move in

John was very professional and a lovely chap. He got straight down to business and left no prisoners (jobs). Straight talking guy, jobs done and not afraid to reschedule or take the time needed, nothing rushed. Very good job much recommended!
great service and staff

Very pleasant and professional staff, made the process so simple and relieved a lot of stress
Student cribs have been friendly and welcome - have helped when things need doing
Jeff is a star! Always goes above and beyond when doing maintenance on our house. Done an amazing job on our bathroom!
Jeff did an amazing job on our bathroom and is always so quick and kind and helpful fixing anything to do with maintenance in our house! We always love to see him coming and does an excellent job 😊
Jeff and Garry are always lovely great chat as well
Jeff & Garry always do an amazing job at fixing our property when ever we need help with something. So polite & always so friendly.
Jeff was great, helped us with our sink, thanks again
Student cribs prompt response to maintenance  is top notch 👌. Jeff especially, is always cheerful and ready to help. Thanks so much guys
Garry and Jeff excellent help came really fast and sorted the issue immediately
Garry was great and got the job done!!

Jeff was very helpful today, always a pleasure. He's done a couple jobs for me in the past, always with utmost efficiency and very respectful.

Would have as a personal handyman if I could / had the need in the future.

Highly recommended, 5 star service

All the staff here are friendly and nice. Jeff and Garry are the 2 main people that do maintenance on my student house and do there best in any situation and are happy to answer any questions and give advice when needed. Whenever there door they are both always polite not only when I have reported an issue but also when I’m not expecting them. More then happy to help them out where I can if it’s making brews or giving a detailed description on the problem I have with the Maintenance knowledge I have to make there job easier in return for the amazing work
Good work by Garry, bath isn't dangerous now :)
Jeff came in today to redecorate our bathroom. The finish on it was exceptional, and he was absolutely brilliant. We can’t thank him enough!
Poor management no WiFi for months on end and the management put it into the students so the provider refuses to talk to them as they are not a party to the contract.
reviews.reply_text:
Dear Ninian,
 
We are sorry to see your review. Our Portfolio Manager has been in contact with all tenants impacted. We understand that the tenants followed the troubleshooting process with our supplier, who could resolve the issue first time. We have now escalated this to our Wi-Fi supplier internally. We fully appreciate the frustration caused by this and the tenants have been provided with temporary Wi-Fi provision in the form of a 4G modem until we are able to fully rectify the issue.
 
We are grateful for the groups continued patience. If we can assist with anything else, please contact us on 0203 758 7000.
 
Thank you,
The Student Cribs Team

reviews.text: I had an amazing experience living in my crib
Very good place for a mature student would definitely recommend.
Very lovely apartment and it really worth the rent fees
The houses are incredibly affordable and having staff on hand to help with issues makes life much more comfortable

Love this place as it is close to Salford University and is very convenient. It is such a safe and comfortable area to reside in as a student and I would recommend! Also, there is free parking for your car and a maintenance team on site to help. Student Cribs is like a home away from home :)

My Experience at Student cribs has been wonderful most especially the staffs, they are very understanding, accommodating and highly polite. For the price is highly competitive compare to the facility you get. Overall, my stay at student cribs has been wonderful
Pretty much what you expect for student housing. Its close to everything, nice enough area
It’s close to uni but paying a lot for what it is especially because our house is seemingly falling apart, but maintenance are doing a good job trying to sort it.
Fantastic team and service, would highly recommend using if you are looking to rent. Quick, Easy and Efficient.
Good area close to the university. Ideal place for couples with lots of space. However rent is on the pricey side and the place is a little outdated

Absolutely love this place and would stay forever if I could. Straight on it when you have a problem and my flat is great. I’ve been able to make it look like home as long as it goes straight back to how it was when I move out. Shoutout to the maintenance guy John, absolutely lovely and always there to help when it’s needed.
reviews.reply_text: Hi Kira, Thank you for your great feedback! We are pleased to hear you are happy with the support you received from our maintenance manager John. Thanks, The Student Cribs Team.

reviews.text: Been here a year 4 months. Good for the price flat, but rent is increasing significantly next year as it no longer includes electric bills. I would expect at least the rent to stay the same with this being the case. It's not quite as good value for money now, but it's a good little uni home
Excellent staffing and support structure. Buildings are old but are being steadily renovated.
Lovely estate, staff at student cribs are friendly and helpful the flats are nice and it’s a good place to live as a student
Really helpful staff and lots of flexibility. Would recommend for students of Salford.
staff are generally helpful, houses have clearly been lived in but for student housing it’s relatively good
All round well place to live, staff are nice and fast if anything needs fixing

While the Crib has a slightly higher rent than some other places, it’s all inclusive price of £115 per week including quick remedy of any issues with the flat is very good value. The internet could be better and the walls need a thorough de-moulding and the tiles in the shower should go to the top — that said, everything is safe and friendly and warm and not having to worry about bills is brilliant. The staff are wonderful.
Very good pricing of flats. Maintenance is always done very quickly.

I lived at Waterside for two years and would not recommend. I felt neglected, overlooked and if anything a nuisance to the maintenance and owner. However, if they actually looked after their homes and kept on top of simple tasks maybe the houses would be in better shape and worth the money. The neglect we experienced resulted in myself, and I know of others, needing medical attention from living in a damp environment and are now stuck with an inhaler and other medication. As well as this, the house was given to us in a filthy and unkept condition. A DIY hole patch up in the living area, broken sofas and mould/damp in almost every room ( especially the top bedrooms). Its an insult to charge so much rent for such living conditions. Our house is without a doubt being left in a much better state than it was given to us and I hope the next residents don’t go through what we have. If the previous management wouldn’t help us we had to take care of issues ourselves to make it a safe living environment. Issues such as the bedrooms being freezing in the winter, waking up with frost on the inside of the window or my face wet from condensation, making us ill, our clothes and bedding wet also from the damp, wet/damp patches on the ceiling, a leak from the bathroom into our living area and tackling damp ourselves.

reviews.text: I'm really happy here I wish I could stay here for another year. Really easy process of signing in Student Cribs staff is really helpful and they on site staff in Salford absolutely amazing. Highly recommend this to everyone

Best part of Waterside village is the staff. Really helpful and quick to respond for maintenance or any other request you might have. Rooms were nice and appliances/facilities fairly new and clean. Everywhere felt safe and secure. Great for parking, always had parking available even for guests plus it was free. Good Covid-19 response - with the opportunity to make a video tour for future tenants and earn £50 off the next rent. Overall, very happy with waterside village and its staff.  Great place to live. Would highly recommend.

Living at waterside this past year was very pleasant and stress-free! I found the communication was very effective and I never had any problems. reviews.text: STAY AWAY FROM THIS PLACE! Appliances are a joke, staff are unfriendly and lazy. Had a shower that leaked through the floor and in to the kitchen every single day because it was so outdated and the layout of the bathrooms are all wrong. Told the management countless times, who tried to fix it but never did. Each time they "fixed" it, they said they would return to paint the kitchen ceiling (without charge) but never did. Moved out a year ago, and since then I have been plagued with emails telling me I owe them money for the kitchen to be painted,  EVEN THOUGH THEY SAID THEY WOULD DO IT COST FREE! All they're trying to do is scam students out of their money, no chance of ever getting a deposit back if you pay one!

DO NOT RENT HERE.
absolute scammers running this place,
moved in and everything seemed okay but then it slowly but surely went down hill as the weeks went on.
the bathroom was absolutely rotten after 2 weeks of staying there and showering we noticed a lot of water was on the floor of the bathroom after further inspection we realized it was a lose pipe under the bath.
we got the facilities guys to come "fix it" free of charge as it was the landlords fault. this was a problem for the entire year we stayed there we called them to fix it over 5 times, eventually it go to the point it leaked through the ceiling and destroyed the kitchen. they kept records of there maintenance and gave us a copy which we unfortunately forgot about and left behind when we left. we left in July and left them keep the deposit for the last months rent, 4 months later we were contacted saying we owed them money for repairs and cleaning, the cleaning was fair enough we agreed to that. but they tried to charge us for painting the kitchen because of the leaks and the bathroom! they threatened court and because we didnt keep our evidence we had to pay.

IF YOU MAKE THE HORRENDOUSLY STUPID DECISION TO RENT HERE TAKE PICTURES OF EVERYTHING AND DOCUMENT EVERYTHING EVEN IF ITS A SCRATCH ON THE TABLE.

House was damp very badly insulated the heating was terrible and there was consistent rattling. the walls are as thin as paper if your neighbor whispers you can hear them.

also a haven for dealers and users alike, the main road outside the compound isnt safe either.

RENT SOMEWHERE ELSE

Amazing and helpful staff, very quick to repair any issues with a 24/7 emergency line during reception closing times (e.g. weekends). I think the value for money is very good considering how clean it is and how good the equipment is (the oven is amazing and actually cooks food, which is something  a lot of other accommodation has lacked in...) Very spacious and couple friendly. Its a nice area too, everyone is friendly, which is lovely. There are bars nearby, a nice walk to Peel Park and a greggs, subway 5 minutes down the road. As well as salford shopping centre (about 35  minutes on foot or 15 minutes
via buses) which has a lot of good shops for students (Iceland, Aldi, Lidl, heron foods, poundland, etc). would recommend over any other accommodation  in Salford. Only downside, which is common for any accommodation is that your neighbors might be loud (depending on your luck). That is not the accommodations'  fault, that's just  common for Student accommodation.
A great place for students. Very safe looking, when I know a number of students who privately rented houses in the area were unfortunately burgled.
Lovely care home with an amazing garden.
Fantastic experience of living here so far! Staff are amazing to help and very friendly. Any maintenance issues get fixed very quickly.
Residential village
Ok

DO NOT EVEN CONSIDER WATERSIDE VILLAGE EVEN IF IT IS YOUR LAST RESORT

I would give this place 0 stars if I could. If this is your last resort for a place to live as a student, do not even consider this place. First off, a mouse occasionally ran around our flat that came in through a hole in the washroom wall that was there when I moved in. The front desk will not be there for you for any requests. My flatmate went to the front desk and asked about heating, and the lady simply laughed and him and gave no response. The walls are incredibly thin. I mean, I could hear my flatmate sit up out of his chair through the wall with both our doors completely closed. Finally, they will try to scam you. Don't expect to get that deposit back even if you left the place nicer than when you arrived. Both my flatmate and I have left our flats in pristine condition and we have yet to receive our security deposits back. In addition, they actually emailed me asking for 800 pounds in fees that I apparently did not pay... I emailed back to ask where these fees came from and they never replied. Seems like shady activity to me, trying to trick students that don't have much knowledge on living on their own for the first time. DO NOT CHOOSE WATERSIDE. I REPEAT. DO NOT.

TERRIBLE SERVICE LACK OF COMMUNICATION!! arranged to see the property twice and been in contact several times being told me and my partner could move into a 1 bedroom house. When it came to signing the contract i was then told we would have to rent a 2 bedroom! this was after driving far to see the 1 bedroom flats and 1 bedroom properties on separate occasions  with my partner and being told we could move in after the second viewing we tried to reach the manger his phone continuously rang out! we both are now homeless for September DO NOT USE THIS SERVICE!
Great accommodation for students
Fantastic accommodation  with a first rate maintenance  team

I strongly advice students not to live in this accommodation. Poor maintenance all year round. Below average 'free wifi' (didn't work all year) and when asked to fix it they try charging us £20 for maintenance. They will rob you of your deposit and claim false damages in the house.
Great place to stay
Worst place ever for a student, don't rent here.
pick somewhere else, this place was a living hell.
This place is a total scam. Don't rent there.
The company who has not paid back my deposit yet since October 2015!
okay rooms, but poor service, not helpful staff.

DO NOT LIVE HERE!

Had so many leaks, mold, lots of spiders, and the office staff are barely in and are reluctant to help you. Absolute con artists. The manager shouts, swears and laughs at tenants who have a complaint - They don't give a flying s***

Not worth your time and money. Students - live in halls or get a house share.  Professionals - Just don't even bother.

Despicable hole.

Wretched, tiny,  out-dated 80's throw up houses.  Smells of the recycling plant (500m away) - dirty, dusty area in general.  Infested with fruit flies, the bins are always over-flowing, spider infestations abound as a result.  Noisy people, noisy road, usual police helicopters every night.  No use having "secure" gating when the residents think it's acceptable to sit on your car at 3am conversing with "friends".

Seriously, I'd sleep rough in Pripyat rather than go back here.  Hateful place - worst year of my life.

Only plus point is that it's close to the motorway to get out of there.

Save your money.

This is simply best place place to live  for second, third year and mature students. A secure gated estate with 127 houses and flats  just 5 minutes walk from Salford university, near shops, bars and restaurants and set in 4 acres of gardens. Free fast wifi, 24/7 CCTV, free parking., free university bus service, free contents insurance. And more than double the space of comparable student digs, for less money.

I contacted Student Cribs twice by live chat and phone to report serious fly-tipping by students moving out from one of their HMOs (among the waste was packaging with the address on to confirm the perpetrator and I confirmed with Student Cribs this was one of their properties).

Student Cribs couldn't have seemed less bothered about the issue and the member of staff on the phone even tried to brush it off and say they would resolve it once the tenants had moved out. So I have to live with waste blowing in the wind around the street where I live until it's convenient for the company to resolve it, do I?

Funnily enough when I challenged her on the fact it never should have been there in the first place she had nothing to say. The local council have made plenty of provision for students to discard their waste properly. No apology was given whatsoever, and I have not had any formal feedback on my report. In the end I believe the council cleared the waste - of course at a cost to the tax payer.

The website could really do with a complaints form where you are given a reference number. And the telephone lines don't give you appropriate options if you are not a student so you have to press and hope for the best.

Overall, really poor and irresponsible property management.

reviews.text: An absolutely fantastic property, equipped with all the amenities a student could need to live comfortably. I've had the pleasure of staying with Student Cribs for two years, and I must say, their commitment to prompt issue resolution is truly commendable. I wholeheartedly recommend Student Cribs, particularly their Rodney Street properties, to anyone looking for a great living experience.
Really friendly staff, always tries their best to sort any issues. I’m definitely booking early for next year :)
Staff onsite and online are very helpful. No question goes unanswered and they genuinely care for your happiness in your crib !!! Unlike other ‘student living’
Very good company. Always been really good at coming out with maintenance

I wish there was a 0 star option. The rudest, most incompetent, least helpful agency I have ever had the displeasure of renting from. Your washing machine/fridge breaks down on a Friday night? Have to wait until Monday to get it fixed as it is classed as “non-emergency” (in a house with 8 people, this is definitely an emergency).

Four months in total we went without wifi and ended up having to buy our own data (when we are already paying an extortionate price for a small room on a student budget).

Our kitchen has been flooded twice now because of the washing machine breaking, and not once have they came to look at this.

Hot water and heat break every other week. And when fixed, it is scolding hot that you would burn your scalp.

Didn’t give us a key for our back gate to take our bins out for months, and we ended up having piles of rubbish outside. Not healthy or hygienic to live around either. Says the house comes with a mop, bucket and hoover - we only got those a couple of months ago and we have been here since September.

And lastly, customer service is non-existent. Staff are rude and ignorant over the phone, with absolutely no consideration for the problems that we have to endure because they don’t want the hassle.

Please avoid and do not rent from this agency, you will get virtually nothing (but stress) for your money.

I have almost finished my year long tenancy with Student Cribs this year and cannot fault a single thing. Any minor maintenance issues we have experienced such as a beeping fire alarm, wifi and broken dryer have been fixed within 24 hours thanks to John our maintenance man. He is amazing and the reason why our stay with Student Cribs this year has been faultless. I would ignore any bad reviews on this company and can only commend them for making this year comfortable and stress free. Have recommended this company to many people.
They don’t listen to tenant’s issues!!
