reviews.text:
In my current experince with Student Cribs, I have found a beautiful spacious house. In which I can relax and  study in peace. However, even with the best of places, there are always maintainance problems that arise.

My heating, stopped working, and in this weather it’s a nightmare and the last thing you need.
I emailed Jessica Blake, the manager and she had the handy man Jay out to the property, within 15 minutes. I was extremely impressed by this. Once Jay had arrived, he came and sorted the issue out swiftly. It was all completed from contacting Jess, to Jay sorting out the heating within less than an hour.

Always very swift with responses! And definitely on the ball with efficiency! I would highly recommend using student cribs. You won’t be disappointed whatsoever!

I want to give a huge thank you and well-deserved recognition to Dale from the property maintenance team.

Dale recently helped repair my wall, and the quality of his work was genuinely outstanding. From start to finish, he showed real professionalism, skill, and attention to detail. The repair itself was done to a very high standard — you would never know there had been any damage in the first place.

What really stood out, though, was his attitude. Dale was friendly, respectful, and clearly takes pride in what he does. He didn’t rush the job and made sure everything was done properly, which really shows his commitment to good workmanship and customer care.

It’s not often you come across someone who combines technical ability with such a positive and helpful approach, but Dale absolutely does. He’s a real credit to the team he works with, and his effort and hospitality deserve to be recognised.

Thank you, Dale, for going above and beyond — genuinely excellent service. Five stars all the way.

A member of the flat has had multiple breaches of her contract, and physically assaulted my guest , I haven’t been able to move back in for over a month and nothing is being done by Student Cribs. Multiple members of the flat have had problems with this resident, involving the police, and we have all complained, it is a terribly unsafe and hostile environment and yet I am still unable to move back in.

I wanted to share a positive experience we had with Nik at Student Cribs.

We had been dealing with an ongoing issue for around a month and a half, which had been quite frustrating. However, once Nik got involved, the situation was handled with real efficiency and professionalism.

We really appreciated his proactive approach and clear communication. It made a frustrating situation much easier to deal with, and we’re grateful for the support he provided.

Thank you, Nik, for your help and dedication.
Dale was very helpful.
Lovely student accommodation. I love the social spaces and the interior, as well as the bedroom sizes and the fact that some of the flats have speakers in each bedroom!

Student cribs are amazing. There flats and houses are great and I would 1000% recommend. The people who run it are so nice and everyone is so friendly. Maintenance is amazing and so quick at responding to issues that are needing to be done. I genuinely couldn’t think of a better place to live. The rooms are big and the shared spaces are amazing. U couldn’t ask for a better company to go with
Spacious rooms and beautiful layout
Have got biohazard waste in my basement, mold and leaks yet they refuse to do anything although the matter is urgent.

I faced some unfortunate circumstances of losing an accommodation which i booked earlier in the year so i could move back to Nottingham after my placement. It was difficult finding accommodation and then i was recommended student cribs. After contacting with student the property manager nick assisted me with showing the flats virtually, explaining me every details about the locality and how close it is from the university, the amenities, the access to the portal, explaining the pricing and how easily nick helped in booking the flat and providing other perks. The student cribs team was very helpful in regards of other processes the free pizza event was great in helping socialise with other students in park view. The location is great gooseberry bush spoons in closer too for drinks socials, it’s affordable unit in terms of pricing and the great thing I find is the inbuilt speak in the room which helps me enjoy my music or movies, i am also glad to find such great flatmates who are caring, understanding and helpful to keep the flat clean, we have fun all the time watching Netflix or going bowling or socials, as Nick suggested me the flat J i am living in, the rooms are decent size . I would recommend park view of you are looking for convenience , comfort and quality of living with good budget.
Amazing student housing, really friendly landlords who were always ready to help.

Hi My daughter moved in her flat in September in Nottingham  the room is very small and the window has been broken the mattress I have called them today and spoke to  Charlie  an said the window will be fixed next week  My only Issues  is if it rains the whole bedding will get wet.  My daughter is Ill now because the room  is freezing please do not use  student cribs

I moved into a Student Cribs Nottingham property on 8th August 2025 (rent was paid on 29th July), and the condition of the house was completely unacceptable. On arrival, there was a pile of rubbish left by the previous tenants right outside, directly in front of Room 1, which made the room stink of old duvets and dirt. No property should ever be handed over like this—especially when rent has already been paid in advance.

Inside, it was even worse:
The house wasn’t freshly painted.
Both bathroom shower cabin doors were broken.
Old and wobbly chairs and tables.
No hot water.
Mould in Room 1.

Spit marks around the house and even inside my wardrobe.

We immediately raised these issues through email, phone calls, and their maintenance portal. I first spoke to Matilda (9th August), who assured us it would be sorted soon. Then on 11th August, I spoke with Angus from their London office, who told me that only the hot water was considered “urgent” and that everything else would be left to the Nottingham branch. (SHOCKING)

Response: "We are very sorry to hear that you are dissatisfied with the quality of the property at 36 Lenton Boulevard. We take all feedback seriously and aim to address any concerns swiftly and professionally." August 10th, Caludia Terry

After 10 days, only the front yard was cleaned. Everything else remains unresolved despite us repeatedly uploading evidence and chasing them. Last week, the bathroom door actually fell on my flatmate, which could have caused serious injury. Still, the repair was only booked for the second week of September.

The mould in Room 1 is a health hazard—I’ve been coughing for weeks, but there has been no response. Meanwhile, we keep getting daily notifications about viewings for an empty room in our house, but no updates on the urgent repairs.

As a full-time professional working 9–5, I don’t usually have time to write reviews, but this experience has been so frustrating that I felt I had to warn others. It’s been almost a month now, and I still can’t properly unpack because of spit marks in my wardrobe.

When I took advise from my colleagues they said, it genuinely feels like Student Cribs take advantage of young tenants, knowing they can get away with providing substandard housing while charging full rent. Hopefully, this review prevents others from going through what we’re currently dealing with.
Really friendly, kept informed from calls all the time, quick and easy process!
Mark - Great service cheers
Mark Mcdaid great job. Quick, friendly, easy 5 star

I would not recommend living here. The amount of issues I have had since moving in August has been outrageous. The boiler wasn't working properly so I had no hot water or heating, and as this would happen several times, I had to rely on the usage of friends for a simple shower (which is a basic necessity) and bundle up with extra layers of clothes and blankets. I have reported my issues both over the phone and to the property manager, but it takes months to get them fixed. Not to mention, I have had mould growing on my windows and beside my bed, which was making me ill. When I initially reported this, I was told it would be sorted swiftly as it is a serious issue. It took them over a month to send someone from the maintenance team to sort it out. There have been silverfish in the flat, my showerhead has a build-up of limescale, the floorboards in my kitchen are loose and the lights inside the fridge don't work. To get rid of the mould, I have been using a dehumidifier but it's expensive and I can't afford to have it on all the time. For the amount of money I have paid, I would expect these issues to be sorted. No compensation was offered and nothing was taken into consideration from the staff working at Student Cribs. I will not be living here next year and would not recommend the company overall. Very poor.
reviews.reply_text:
Hi Jacey,

We are sorry to see your review. We can see that your property manager has visited your property and discussed this issue with you, and we will continue to work with you to rectify any outstanding issues.
 
Regarding the hot water, we replaced the boilers for the block in November, which means we should not run into any issues with hot water moving forward. 

Please be aware your contract includes all your utility bills, so there will be no additional cost to you for the dehumidifier that we have provided for use in the property.

Please do feel free to call us on 0203 758 7000 to discuss any further issues, so we can ensure these are resolved. 

Thank you, 
The Student Cribs Team

reviews.text:
We’ve had nothing but problems since moving in. From awful cleaning standard since we moved in. To leaking ceilings in both our bedrooms and kitchen. Then no hot water for days each week because they refused to invest in a new boiler despite this clearly being a problem in previous years as seen in the other bad reviews of this property. We are yet to have had any of this resolved. With PPM surveys being stopped this year they clearly just want to profit more from our extortionate rent. Maybe if they cared enough about their tenants this all could have been prevented. However, unfortunately we have been left with no idea of what has been done about these issues, no goodwill gestures or compensation for the living conditions we’ve had to put up with. Each time we call they apologise and say they will be in contact about next steps, then unsurprisingly we never hear back, this having been on repeat since September. It’s safe to say we are now just fed up and I would not recommend this property to anyone.

Update: months later we have been left with absolutely no improvements other than the upstairs bathroom which has been sectioned off to prevent further leaking. The ceiling is still damp and stained. The toilet still smells from the awful plumbing and flush that half works. We have received no compensation.
After multiple calls we were strung along to believe they were taking our situation seriously due to weeks of hot water issues, cleaning and plumbing issues, flooding in both bedrooms and kitchen, along with numerous other problems. We were informed there would be compensation for these inconveniences and that they were waiting for our ceiling to be repaired, so that the compensation would cover the timeframe the maintenance issues had taken to be resolved. However, weeks later I received a phone call to "follow up any issues", absolutely nothing had been done in this time. Another few weeks have passed and a flat mate has received a phone call where we have been informed the work in the kitchen still has not been scheduled and that they were hoping to do in the Christmas break. If we had not asked we would never have received this information. So surely the compensation is now going to cover up to the Christmas break when they are apparently planning to carry out the maintenance work? No. We have been told there is a potential £20 gift voucher. What an absolute joke!! They string us along for weeks just to waste time because they know we are students who only have a 52 week tenancy and if they can prolong it until then they get away with it. This has become more and more apparent as the time has gone on and it's so disappointing. I would never recommend this property, student cribs clearly does not want to invest in maintaining and/or repairing damage. Do not live here.👎
reviews.reply_text:
Hi Eva, 

We are sorry to see your review. Our team have been looking into this for you and your Property Manager has been in touch with you regarding the issues you raised. 

At Student Cribs, we are committed to maintaining the highest standards of cleanliness within all our properties and ensuring the properties are well maintained. As discussed previously the hot water issues have since been resolved with new boiler installation to prevent further instances of loss of hot water, we are sorry for the issues faced and for the length of time it took for this to be resolved. 

As discussed previously, we can confirm the PPM visits are still ongoing , and will continue for us to assess individual properties and ensure they are kept to Student Cribs standards. 

We apologise for the inconvenience you have faced with the leaks caused by the above flat. Thank you for confirming there are no further leaks into your flat and we would like to further thank you for your continued patience while we complete the remedials in your property.

Should you want to run over this in any further detail over the phone, we are of course available.

Thank you, 
The Student Cribs Team

reviews.text: I have currently lived here for 1 month and I cannot begin to list the amount of issues we’ve had within this time. From silverfish to leaks to disgusting cleaning standards before we moved in we’ve had it all. And ontop of that no hot water 3 times now , THREE in the space of a month. With 8 girls living here that is just beyond an inconvenience. After paying them 18k in total before we all moved in to having all of these issues is an utter disgrace. AVOID if you’re looking for your next student property .

reviews.text: We have had nothing but problems since moving into park view! The first major problem is the hot water, we had to go for weeks with no hot water which meant we couldn’t shower or do our washing up. It took several attempts to get a hold of someone from student cribs and they did absolutely nothing to help other than suggest to use a local gym as a place to shower which in the middle of winter is less than ideal when it is freezing cold outside. The hot water problem has continued throughout our tenancy as they will not put in the money to fix the problems long term, only temporarily. The heating is also useless, our bedrooms become freezing cold and instead of fixing the problem they have given us cheap useless fan heaters. The windows let in a huge draft as they do not close properly leading to lots of condensation. The WiFi has also been down for a week now, which for university students with work and exams to be doing is a major problem and when trying to get in contact with student cribs about it they didn’t care and we were told to solve the problem ourselves and get in contact with the WiFi provider, when we did this we were on the phone for an hour and the only solution reached was for us to use our own mobile data and they will not give us any compensation for this either when in our contract the WiFi is meant to be included! Also as I am writing this our washing machine is filling up our kitchen sink with black water. I would honestly not recommend this accommodation to anyone, it is one problem after the next with no permanent solutions. Park view is worse than a first year student accommodation with paper thin walls you can hear absolutely everything. For just shy of £180 a week living here has been the biggest waste of money and not one thing has made this place worth it. With all these problems in such a short space of time the only compensation we have received is less than £100 which is an absolute joke considering what we pay. We receive numerous emails a week from student cribs which are just filled with useless information or apologies for the problems that are occurring but no solutions. Student cribs is a rip of and they take advantage of young students who they think don’t know any better. We deserve compensation for all the problems we have had to deal with . Absolute joke!!!!
reviews.reply_text:

We are sorry to see your review. Your Portfolio Manager has been trying to contact you to discuss the matters raised. Can you kindly call us on 0203 758 7000 so that we can speak with you.

Thanks,
The Student Cribs Team

reviews.text:
Currently living in a student cribs property and unfortunately leaving a review feels like the only option to get some better support and service!
Park View has had no consistent and reliable hot water for a month now, this never seems to get fully fixed and within a few days we are back to cold showers. Paying for hot water but not receiving it is a joke & a small compensation fee does not justify it. Our electric radiators are not working either, so the flat is very cold and now hitting the colder weather we are struggling to get warm after going out. I have had to make trips home to warm up and have hot showers. We have made a maintenance job for our toilet (issue 3) which someone is not coming for 3 weeks-a bit of a joke. Issue 4 has been ongoing for months now but got severely worse-the parking. Those with cars have Student Cribs our number plates , 12 cars for 12 spaces. However I have given my number plate and have no where to park, resorting in paying for parking on the streets. This is unfair and your customer service assistant (Esen) was unhelpful, stating this was my issue and I had to find out which cars were/were not meant to be in the parking lot, this is impossible and Student Cribs responsibility as they have the number plates. So sending empty threats saying you will ticket cars without registered parking is not cutting it and something needs to change.
As I am writing this email, I am stood outside freezing as the fire alarm has been going off for approx 40 minutes without anyone attending.
The flat is lovely, modern and we were so excited to live here however the mounting issues are getting ridiculous. Please sort these issues out as I did not want to resort to publicly announcing these issues but feel it is the only option.

reviews.text: We paid for aerial TVs in both the living room and the bedrooms that weren’t fixed for months, and weren’t responded to when we asked for reimbursement. The chromecast that was “provided” in the booklet also cost us money to use, which we were then told we shouldn’t have needed to pay for, but we were yet again not reimbursed for that. The washing machine and tumble dryer were both broken when we moved in and took weeks to be replaced. The internet is incredibly unreliable, and because of the company that Park View currently uses you have to pay extra money to add devices like chromecasts in the bedrooms. The plumbing is terrible, our main bathroom pipes make a horrendous noise on the daily. I am living in another Student Cribs property next year, and after viewing the property and talking to tenants currently living in the building I’m moving into, I do believe the issues me and my flatmates have experienced are strictly related to Park View. I would recommend student cribs, especially since they give you priority access to viewings for the next year if you live in one of their properties, but definitely check reviews for the individual properties before you book with them. They are overall an alright company else I wouldn’t be living with them again, but Park View is a shambles.

reviews.text: Very good service from Melanie and her team in Nottingham, every query we had as tenants was sorted promptly and without any hassle, would definitely recommend renting from them!
Great experience with Student Cribs!  My house was amazing! Thanks a lot to the team.
I love this flat! It’s gorgeous, the decor is super modern and luxury!

I have really enjoyed my time at Student Cribs, such a modern and homely property. Viewing the properties was such an easy process and reserving one was made easy by the fantastic time!

All the staff are so friendly and maintance work was carried out promptly. Health and safety checks are done regularly as well as inspections every term. Plenty of warning when someone’s expected to visit the property too.

Highly recommend, only downside is that it’s very pricey but you only get what you pay for!
Within 1 min walk from the middle of NTU, great for studying and only having to get out of bed 5 min before your lecture starts. High end accommodation and good location.

Great experience living in Student Cribs accommodation with friendly staff,if there is a problem they deal with it very efficiently depending on the problem. Although pricey for student accommodation, it is definitely higher quality than the other houses in Nottingham. So if you have the money, definitely worth the extra bit.

Have had such a good experience with student cribs, they are very efficient, if there is a problem they deal with it straight away. The flat is so luxurious and modern. Couldn’t of chosen a better place to live.

I have enjoyed my year living in student cribs accommodation. With friendly staff which are quick to reply and a reliable maintenance team, it has meant that I haven't had to worry about anything house related and I could focus on my studies.

Although the accommodation is pricey, I would recommend it to students as the business make everything stress free and comfortable

Everything you would expect form high-end student accommodation. Any issues with the flat were resolved very quickly. Would recommend to anyone else looking for accommodation close to Nottingham Trent University.

Thoroughly enjoyed living at student cribs this year, due to the amount of space we have we were able to host the majority of the time. However, music complaints are ridiculous, would have security come in multiple times in a night when we were just watching tv. There were some problems with the kitchen, although it was sorted out promptly with maintenance coming within a day.

I have thoroughly enjoyed living in a student cribs flat this year, the interiors are decorated to a very high quality and bedrooms spacious with every appliance you could need. We are always having friends over due to the amount of space we have in our kitchen/living room. Everyone loves it here. We have had a few issues with our kitchen, however maintenance have been sent out that evening/the next day to fix the issues, and would happily come back if there were any other issues, very happy with how quickly things will be fixed!
Watch out for security though, they will happily just let themselves into your flat without ringing the door bell/knocking (once at 5am when we were all asleep).
AWFUL COMPANY AND RUDE STAFF. DON'T GO WITH THEM.

If I could rate student cribs less than 1 star I would.
Student cribs have caused me persistent stress over my uni experience. They claim to prioritise students but really try and squeeze money out of us where possible. whether it's isolating someone in your group to pay equally for a lot less, or forcing you out of your accomodation because one tenant has finished their degree...

The companies ethics really need to be revised on a smaller scale to help ensure students are prioritised more.

Student cribs are the most awkward company I have ever dealt with - they try and get away with charging you as much money as possible, the deposits they require you to pay are massive (£675!). When you want to book a house and get them to sign a contact they make you pay £200 - this £200 took 6 months to be returned to me. Not to mention that all of their contracts are 52 weeks! They will try and trick you out of as much money as possible.

They also have made my student life difficult, I am SURE the CEO Charlie Vaughan-Lee would not wish for his company to be like this. I wish I could have left a better view.

We have recently worked with Student Cribs on one of their projects. They are a great company to deal with, very efficient, prompt payments, as well as having an interest in innovative products and design.
Anytime I’ve had an issue with my room the maintenance team Dave and Harry have been really efficient when it came to getting the job done and also are really lovely
Great service from the maintenance team. Dave and Harry fixed my front door and it works perfectly now. Came on time, got it sorted quickly and cleaned up after too. Really appreciate it!
Harry and Dave they were awesome maintenance team good job and upto the expectations.
Very informative and helpful with the moving process

Best place to live in very modern and clean,
student crip is so professional and helpful at anytime especially Sheryl c property manger in Leicester very helpful and lovely women.

Sheryl Cheadle is the best (and i mean BEST!!) landlord ever! She has been extremely helpful and kind to me during my tenancy, and has always helped with any issues i had. The application process was super smooth thanks to her.
So a massive thank you to Sheryl, she needs a pay raise and promotion, she is an absolute gem that deserves the whole world!!!!

Seearthing for a place was very daunting but Charlotte literally walked me through the entire process. I did initially have some issue  with my viewing but besides that the team was extremely polite and sweet.

amazing accom, amazing rates and appliances. any issues throughout the year have been addressed and resolved within a week. massive props to george and jack. seeing them sorting everything out day in day out
Good communication, nice accommodations,
Unbelievably bad service. They value money over anything

reviews.text: Student cribs flat is what you make it to be, the interior design of the flat is very modern and sanitary. Me and my friends have lived there the past year we have made a conscious effort to keep the flat clean which makes the flat more enjoyable to live in and keeps its looking nice. The maintenance team have been incredible and fix all issues as quickly as possible. When your wanting a sanitary, homely looking place to live with your friends during your uni academic year I recommend student cribs.
Good experience here and everything works perfectly, maintenance team are always on it so would recommend

Really happy with my experience at The Hosiery Factory managed by Student Cribs!

The Room & Facilities:
My room is spacious, clean, and has everything a student needs. The building is modern with excellent facilities including Dishwasher, washing machine, Dryer, reliable WiFi, and secure access. 24/7 Support. Location is perfect - close to DMU campus and the city centre.

The Management:
Krizia has been incredibly helpful since I moved in & joined as a Student Brand Manager. She's always quick to respond and helps resolve any issues smoothly. Her friendly approach and clear communication make everything so much easier. Really appreciate having someone so reliable and supportive when you need assistance.

Would definitely recommend to other students looking for quality accommodation with excellent support!
Harry and Dave are amazing

Where do I begin? There wasn’t any heating since the day we moved in, and maintenances solution was to give us all old space heaters, there was no hot water as well. The washing machine was broken and it took TWO AND A HALF MONTHS for it to be replaced. During Christmas break, there was a leak in the pipes and it caused a small flood, we also had mould in our kitchen because of a leak in the flat above ours. The biggest problem was the squatters and break-ins, the lock for the front door to the building was intentionally jammed, the lock did get replaced but our keys didn’t work, it’s very stressful when the fire alarm goes off and the main door for the building won’t open. When the lock got replaced again, the fire alarm went off again and kept going off for hours because the firefighters couldn’t get in because the property manager was asleep. A few months later the lock was completely drilled off, which caused squatters to camp out in the boiler room. And those were only the big problems, there’s a bunch more. One of the cupboard shelves was broken and it took a month for maintenance to fix it. The toilet seat wasn’t secure for months, the shower knob was loose.
The worst part is maintenance’s response, they report jobs as completed even though they haven’t been completed, and trying to contact them is almost impossible. Definitely not worth the amount of money we payed to stay.

Absolutely loved living in student cribs the past year. It’s in the perfect location to get to and from university and go into town. The communal space is perfect for you and your friends. I have also experienced that with any maintenance issues they are fixed quick and efficiently. Would really recommend student cribs to any student in Leicester
Amazing accommodation for your money's worth. Just no reception so kinda feels like you're on your own

I love living with my friends. I feel safe in my flat and it is really affordable. I love that I can either sit in the lounge and watch TV or in my room. The washing machine and dishwasher are a major bonus and super convenient. I am so glad that I moved into student cribs.
very beautiful flats, loved living with my friends, it is in a good location close to DMU and only a short walk into town
I am staying in student cribs for last one and half years.It was really nice experience . It was neat, clean and well furnished. I really like to stay here. Also easily approachable to everywhere.

Dave and Harry are so quick to respond to any maintenance problems
Dave and harry sorted our new fridge within a matter of hours. Great maintenance team !
Thanks to Dave and Harry, super helpful
Dave and harry are legends, they will have you sorted at all times.

Thank you for making this transition so seamless. I’m looking forward to a great year ahead!

so far all good

Friendly understanding management, that works with standers and professional ethics also place interest of their stakeholders at heart,I got two heater that was brought to my room am going to use one and return one,

Avoid this place like the plague (continuation)
So we were there for 2 weeks without heating and we asked if they would at least give us some heaters temporarily. They agreed on the phone but nobody showed up or supplied us with anything. That house in the winter got cold enough to see our breath inside. I don’t have an exact temp but it was cold.
On the inverse of this however, the windows don’t open due to the safety measures in place. In the summer heat wave, I hope you bring air conditioning or a fan because you will pass out in your hallways. Dangerous levels of heat in that building. Often hotter than the 35/40 degree weather outside.
The internet drops out inconsistently and is great for watching youtube or tv. However it takes ages to download or upload anything of moderate size, be it video games, university work or large files. And the internet inconsistently drops out so frequently that it is impossible to do any live events or games that require the internet. Sometimes even messaging apps such as discord, Skype, teams drop out mid call.
There is no parking allowed on premises so kiss your car goodbye or risk parking it on the street with the crack heads and drug dealers that walk outside the gate.

This one is less to do with the staff and more with the other tenants but the walls are pretty thin, so expect to hear conversations, loud music, washing machines or loud tv’s through walls. And if you have your window open, expect the unpleasant smog of weed stank and the sound of singing to permeate your own living space.

There have also been some occasions in which the staff, (student cribs and 3rd party companies employed by student cribs) have on multiple occasion entered the flat without notice. It has been a huge invasion of privacy and an unpleasant one at that. They are meant to issue you 24 hrs via email or text before they complete a house viewing or maintenance regarding your building and have failed to do so time and time again. Getting as far as entering our rooms while we slept to give a house tour to random people. This is not only illegal but fairly worrying.
On top of this during viewings multiple items had been misplaced or stolen from our rooms, and not only us. Somebody had taken shoe laces, keyboard caps, an entire Nintendo switch just to name a few. Some other tenants from other parts of the block had noticed the same, none of it compensated or replaced by the company.

The fire alarms would be set off multiple times from people smoking, vaping or cooking in their house. It’s sets off the whole complex and we aren’t able to silence it ourselves. Neither can the fire department. It rarely gets silenced within the hour it first goes off so expect to stand outside in the cold for hours at a time in your pyjamas waiting for a lazy staff member to drive down too quiet the building so you can resume your work/sleep. Honestly we have had times where we would rather walk to Wetherspoons for a couple hours to have a meal and drinks, come home and the alarm is still blaring.

Honestly the only good I can think about this place was the location as its close to town and the university so typically you can walk just about anywhere you need fairly quickly.

Don’t risk it. It may be more pricey elsewhere but I will never recommend this place or business to anyone.

AVOID THIS PLACE LIKE THE PLAGUE!!!
I have lived here for the past year and my experience has been horrific.
The Staff are slow to act and unhelpful. The online support is borderline non existent. They leave the building in poor shape, messy and sometimes ignore your problems completely. The advertised “fully furnished” rooms are a lie. There’s so many issues.

From the moment we moved in we were lacking basic equipment and rooms that were promised to us. Our move in was pushed back 5 weeks due to them “renovating the room in which we were to be staying”. (For background context there was 5 of us in the flat, and only bathroom so they said that they were going to install a new one). Once we got there, they had not only somehow not installed a bathroom, but the kitchen was the only thing that had been renovated in the first place and poorly at that. The curtains weren’t hung, all of the bedrooms had old tattered stained carpets. The doors were worn and old. The furniture we did have was coated in paint from the poor job of the staff after painting the walls. We had one working fridge among the 5 of us. Even in the kitchen they miss cut the flooring and ended up using a nail gun to staple the laminate flooring down instead of cutting it to size.

We were missing so many supplies and even after asking for an inventory list or replacement gear for months, we were left with empty promises and had to buy our own. We were missing a dustpan and brush, multiple rubbish bins, a kettle, mop and bucket, sound bar (which should come included with the TV), Toilet brush, Multiple mirrors, entire dressers, a secondary fridge etc, etc. Some of the staff even commented when viewing the suite and said that we were missing the equipment listed but they didn’t do anything about it.

Onto the staff when dealing with issues. They tend to be slow to complete jobs no matter how urgent or how long in advance you remind them of the issues at hand. You can message the staff through 3 main ways, face to face, through the phone, or via email/the student portal. All as useless as the last.
We have had issues with the washer/dryer breaking mid cycle and leaving our clothes stuck within the washer for literal weeks before they helped us remove our gear. Our fire doors were deemed not fire safe at the start of the year, by the time we were moving out they still hadn’t changed them. We’ve had multiple issues of rat infestations due to the lack of bin collection and nothing was done. Towards the end of our stay we even had multiple homeless people enter through the locked gate somehow and they ended up smoking meth on the premises and pissing on the walls, continuing to throw garbage on the floors from out of the bins. The police were called on 2 occasions.
Security is non existent. No cameras, no head office, no security staff. This also means that you have to sit at the front gate if you want any chance of collecting a parcel unless you send it to a local pick up point at a post office.
Furthermore, they have a 24hr emergency call operator for out of hours service that are meant to help with emergencies. They don’t work on site or within Leicester's area, so everything that they do has to be emailed in and you are once again stuck waiting for days. One of my housemates locked himself out of his room and couldn’t remember the combination. Luckily due to a few YouTube tutorials we we’re able to get it open ourselves. However we had reported the issue straight away and we’re left with nobody even acknowledging the issue that they had been locked outside. For over a week we waited just to see if anything would be done, but no. Nothing. My flat mate had we not been able to get into his room would have been left to sleep on the couch or in one of our rooms for over a week.

During the winter period, our heating throughout the entire flat didn’t work. The thermostat was broken when we moved in and nothing was done about it. We were promised that it would be sorted within the week. Nothing. Then they said next week. Nothing.

And now we have mice in the flat, it took them three days to have someone come out to start to resolve this, this left me and my flatmates without somewhere to live for three days. Would not recommend. It’s a shame really as it has potential to be a nice flat.

Living at the Shoe and Boot Factory with Student Cribs was hands down one of the worst experiences I’ve ever had. From start to finish, the entire stay was filled with stress, neglect, and a complete lack of professionalism.

Someone broke into our flat, and even after reporting it, nothing was done. There was no compensation, no lock replacement, and absolutely no regard for our safety. We felt completely vulnerable living there. To make matters worse, there was no security on-site at any point, which only made the situation more frightening.

Maintenance was a nightmare. Repairs took forever—our fridge took over a month to be fixed, the dishwasher never worked the entire time we were there (over two years), and flooding in the flat was handled slowly and poorly. There were also rats and bed bugs in the property, which made it feel completely unsafe and unhygienic.

One of the worst parts was the mould growing in the bathroom. It was left untreated for so long that several of us got sick, and it genuinely affected our health. This was not just inconvenient—it was dangerous, and the lack of urgency from management was appalling.

The fire alarm went off every single day for a while, adding even more chaos and stress. And when we tried to raise any of these issues with management, we were met with rude, dismissive, and condescending responses. Communication was awful, and we never felt listened to.

This accommodation is absolutely not worth the money. If I could give it zero stars, I would. Student Cribs needs to seriously sort out how they run their properties. I would never recommend staying here to anyone — not even for a short stay. Stay away.
Harry and Dave were incredibly friendly and approachable. They addressed and resolved the issues in our flat immediately.

Living in the Shoe and Boot Factory with Student Cribs was a terrible experience. The service was awful and maintenance was always slow. Our fridge broke and it took them over a month to fix it. The dishwasher was broken for two years and never got fixed. We had floods in our flat which were eventually dealt with, but not quickly and not properly.

There were rats and bed bugs in the property, which made it feel completely unsafe. We also had mould growing in the bathroom for ages before anything was done about it. Communication was poor and we never felt listened to. Even when things were eventually fixed, it was always too late and never good enough.

Not worth the money at all. Zero out of ten experience. Would not recommend.

Do not look at the flats if you want a stress free time . We are all sick of the time spent on emails and calls , lies and incompetence . Very uncaring disgusting treatment of uni students . The accommodation is well-maintained, clean, and offers a perfect balance between privacy and community living. The fully equipped kitchens are a great feature, and the large TV in the kitchen adds to the social atmosphere, making it a great place to relax and connect with others.

The location is excellent, with easy access to the university, shops, and transport links. The staff are friendly, approachable, and always ready to assist, ensuring a smooth and enjoyable stay.

Overall, The Shoe & Boot Factory is a great place to live as a student, and I highly recommend it to anyone looking for high-quality student accommodation.

The rent in student cribs is favourable and very affordable. One other key thing I enjoy is the maintenance team who is always available to handle all issues.

Genuinely the absolute WORST student 'accommodation' in the whole of LEICESTER!!
The people that run this place do not care about the residents at all. Tiny rooms, nothing ever works, even no hot water for 2 entire weeks (which is illegal). Still no heating (also illegal). The day we moved in, the previous residents belongings were waiting for us, creeped me out. There are dozens of better accommodations for cheaper. This place is an absolute joke. Please, I'm begging you, do not even think about coming here, AT ALL. Is really 0/5 stars, infact -4000/5 stars, 4000 being my rent.

reviews.text: There was so much to complain about and they don't respond to almost anything. Very quick to threaten fines without actually holding up their end of contracts. We have had no heating since the beginning. It took months ths to get the washing machine with rotting soap inside from the previous tenants removed and replaced. Our oven hasn't worked for weeks now. And none of these issues (bar the washing machine) have been fixed. We went weeks with no hot water (compensated). But since the beginning, the shower only had 2 settings. Boiling and freezing and it flipped on a whim. That was never fixed. The showers have mould along the top from the previous tenants and I had to deep clean everything upon moving in as the bathrooms were not cleaned before we moved in. However, the mould went untreated as I lacked the agility to remove ceiling mould myself.

reviews.text: Honestly the worst company I’ve ever lived with. The shower didn’t work from the move in date (September 9th) until January the 14th. It was a simple fix to change the shower unit but the maintenance never showed up or student cribs cancelled the jobs. Then tonight, the fire alarm goes off, we ring out of hours maintenance to say please can you send someone to turn it off as was done before because it is obnoxiously loud. There is no smoke outside or smells of smoke. The out of hours phone number, I wish I had the man’s name, was so rude to me down the phone he told me to call the fire brigade and I said ‘we haven’t done this before’ he says it’s not his problem and that we have to call them. I said that doesn’t seem right  to me. He laughs down the phone at me and tells me to call them and that he cannot do anything about it. It was the worst experience I’ve had. There was no being nice about it it was plain rude and arrogant. Living with this company and in this building is a huge mistake and even the price hasn’t made it worth it because of everything that has gone wrong and was promised and hasn’t been delivered.
Interior design is decent
Well refurnished place
Really nice place cheap and comfortable

Worst student accommodation I’ve been in, in 4 years. Starting off with moving into a half furnished flat and dirty carpets (when communicated this would be decorated brand new). To then being burgled and only after, having window alarms given to us to stick on the windows. Then, being without hot water in both showers for 3 weeks (Did get a reimbursement after the problem was fixed). But now, we are currently without one working shower for the past 6 weeks… we were told they would be fixing it today (5th December) however, no one came and are still waiting on this to be resolved.

reviews.text: If 0 stars was an option I would definitely give them that. Worst accommodation I’ve ever stayed at. Someone was living in my room over summer seemed like a contractor or builder and it took them all the way till the start of university which was 3rd October to clean my room and I still had to pay my first rent in full. I was also told that everything would be brand new and I had massive stains in my carpet. Took them up until the end of October to sort it out. A piece of the wall randomly fell off in the kitchen which could have hurt someone and it was reported to maintenance which was ignored so we had a hole in the wall with the pipes exposed it’s only when maintenance came to our flat to fix the broken door handle a month later that they saw it and put it back. Don’t stay here don’t let the £99 a week persuade you because you will want to leave when you want to they won’t allow you to unless you find someone to take over your tenancy. Other people have leaks in their flat and they basically say that they take no responsibility for the damage of your goods from robberies or leaks.

reviews.text: This has been the worst accommodation and experience in the 3yrs I've been studying here. I've had so many problems & reported so many faults and the staff are unhelpful and don't really care about my or my flatmates welfare. There has been a leak coming through the light in the kitchen for a week now and it started to smoke we called the fire brigade and they were shocked that the flat was signed off as habitable and concerned about our health and safety a week on and the leak has now travelled to our TV in the dining room and no one has come to even look at it let alone fix it. Other faults reported include the heating which doesn't work, a toilet that doesn't flush, broken kitchen drawers, no cupboards in my bedroom, old carpet when I was promised brand new carpet, no kettle, toaster or microwave to name just a few faults. I was not greeted by anyone on the day I moved in which meant I was under house arrest and could not leave the flat, I wasn't provided with an accurate code to access my bedroom, I'm really dissapointed that a large company treats students this poorly charge full rent, yet take no ownership of any of the issues raised. DON'T STAY HERE THE PLACE IS NOT HABITABLE AND THERE ARE SERIOUS CONCERNS OVER HEALTH AND SAFETY MEASURES. If 0 stars was an option I would have selected it.

The new student cribs staff are nice and friendly. However the building itself has so many faults for the past 3 months we had to regularly shower in cold water. Constant viewings which is really frustrating especially during exam season. And poor wifi.

reviews.text: The fire alarm keeps going off in the middle of the night every now and then. There is no way to stop it except to call the emergency number. You also have to pay extra credit on your phone to call the emergency number (which is a different issue in itself)  and wait for someone to respond. The management has some really good people, who make the experience much better. However, some are just rude and not cooperative at all. The fire alarm once or twice can be seen as a mistake by another tenant. However when it happens at 4 am on a Sunday morning for the third time that week, this to me is outrageous. What’s worse is when you call them, they Do not have a hint of guilt or apology. I called them to complaint and I didn’t even hear the man say sorry for the inconvenience once. Ridiculous. I wouldn’t stay here again if I had a choice. Don’t be swayed by quirky interior this building has, it won’t matter when you can’t sleep in your own flat.

reviews.text: Want to sleep past 8? Don’t bother living here as the maintenance men are as loud as they can be, throwing things in the skips and shouting. Want to sleep AT ALL? Sleep deprivation torture comes to mind. Don’t bother to chase it up with student cribs. They don’t care. They’ll leave you to rot, just because you’re students. Just don’t bloody bother. reviews.text: A good place to stay from the Streatham campus, around a 10 minute walk from there. Overall, the property is good, with a few issues regarding the kitchen appliances which are fixed in adequate time by maintenance. However, it's a bit overpriced since the property is located near the city centre. I was satisfied with my stay here and would recommend this place to students who'd like to be conveniently located near the city centre as well as the campus.

reviews.text: The location is absolutely perfect. Groceries and all other stores and cafes are near. University’s business school is a 15 min walk which in my opinion would be the same if you lived on campus accommodations near st. David’s and cardiac hill (the walk from isca isn’t hilly). It’s nice compared to other accommodation. However, there’s less storage in kitchen and bedrooms. The walls aren’t that sound proof. The building is a bit old so it keeps having its issues now and then, hopefully they fix this when they refurbish this. Maintenance comes at a reasonable time if there’s a problem. Communal area has not that many things, there’s a pool and table tennis and a lounge area with study space which I believe contradicts the purpose of study area. Could’ve been more for the price. Overall, It’s a decent place to stay. Just a bit overpriced for the size and storage due to its location.

reviews.text: Been here the last 2 years and it's a great place. Very friendly reception staff, in an ideal location between both campuses and a quick walk to the high street shops. Definitely would recommend.

Excellent customer service, staff are friendly and welcoming.
Always happy to help out.

This is a really great place to stay. I am staying here from last three months and I must say the facilities they are providing is worth each assistance is available all the time and I enjoy staying here. Worth considering 🤟

I have lived here for a year and totally loved my experience. First of all this is the ideal location to stay as it is in the city center and close to the university as well. You have plenty of shops in just 2 minute walking distance and the main bus stand for outstation buses is just 5 minute walk from here.
The accommodation manager Rebecca is extremely friendly and kind and makes sure you have a comfortable stay. If you face any maintenance issue they fix it immediately within 24 hours and makes sure the common space is clean and tidy. The heating system works very well and you will always stay warm [this is really important during the winters]. During covid they were kind enough to offer a full refund if we had vacated the room unlike other accommodations. There are 4 secure doors to your room so safety is definitely not an issue. Compared to other student accommodations they are very reasonably priced for the location and facilities. I have had no negative experiences and totally enjoyed my stay at Isca Lofts and recommend it to you.

Now there's no 'rate us 5* for a 'prize" I can be honest about our time here.

The house is alright but the management is lethargic in organising maintenance unless it would affect them financially, lesser then how it affects us living there. Stuff we've reported on day 1 is not done, several items/minor gaff repairs outstanding and the damage reports are all online on their portal.

Also the parking system was promised to be rectified but hasn't changed from everyone gets threatened a fine regardless if they've a car or not. This was especially salt in wound rubbing when they ridiculed me personally despite my car being street parked at the time.

Overall the experience is good, but if you're going there be wary of how cash strapped the management appear to be. Not a cheap house though compared to now many rivals. Nice though.
